revision.text:
{{Double image aside|right|Emperor Naruhito (cropped).jpg |209| Emperor Akihito 199011 1.jpg |214|Left: Emperor [[Naruhito]] wearing the ''KЕҚrozen no hЕҚ'' (й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ) in the first year of the Reiwa era (2019).
Right: Emperor [[Akihito]] (at the time) wearing the ''KЕҚrozen no hЕҚ'' in 1990 (Heisei 2).}} '''KЕҚrozen no hЕҚ''' (й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ) is the ceremonial [[court dress]] of the [[Emperor of Japan]] since the [[Heian period]]. Worn during enthronement ceremonies and significant rituals, this ''hЕҚ'' (robe) is exclusively reserved for the Emperor and is dyed in the distinctive ''kЕҚrozen'' color. ''KЕҚrozen'' is a dye made from the bark of the [[wax tree]] and the heartwood of [[sappanwood]]. Its hue is described as a reddish-yellow{{Sfn|Nihon Kokugo Daijiten|2000}} or a yellowish-brown{{Sfn|Takada|1986}}. The shade varied depending on the time period and the wearer's age{{Sfn|Suzuki|1985}}. During the [[2019 enthronement ceremony]], foreign media described the color as {{lang|ja|brown-gold}}[https://www.bbc.com/news/world-asia-50120563 "Naruhito: Japan's emperor proclaims enthronement in ancient ceremony"], BBC, October 22, 2019.. == History and Origin == The ''[[YЕҚrЕҚ Code]]'' of the [[Nara period]] regulated [[formal dress]] for the [[Crown Prince]] and officials but did not specify the attire for the Emperor{{Sfn|Kurosaka|1939|p=213}}. The ''[[ShЕҚsЕҚin]] documents'' reveal that the Emperor's court attire (''kon-e'') included ''hakuno awasehЕҚ,'' a white silk robe{{Sfn|Imperial Household Museum|1929|p=65}}{{Sfn|KondЕҚ|2019|loc=В§ 1.2 (Kindle)}}. The ''kЕҚrozen no hЕҚ'' became established as imperial attire in the 11th year of the [[KЕҚnin era]] (820 CE). [[Emperor Saga]] decreed on the first day of the second month that ''kЕҚrozen'' robes be worn for specific occasions such as [[new moon]] ceremonies, [[listening to petitions]], receptions for foreign envoys, [[hЕҚbei]] rituals, and seasonal banquets. For [[Shinto]] rites or visits to [[imperial tombs]] in winter, ''haku-e'' (white robes) were designated; for [[New Year's]] celebrations, ''konmen-jЕ«nishЕҚ'' (еҚҒдәҢз« , twelve-embroidered robes) were used{{Sfn|Nihon Kiryaku|loc=KЕҚnin 11}}. These regulations reflected influences from [[Tang Dynasty]] [[China]], where yellow robes symbolized imperial authority. The ''kЕҚrozen'' hue was believed to represent the sun at its zenith, symbolizing imperial divinity{{Sfn|Takada|1986}}. == Design and Usage == ### Dyeing Process The ''[[Engishiki]]'' describes the materials for dyeing ''kЕҚrozen'' robes: 14 ''kin'' of wax tree bark, 11 ''kin'' of sappanwood, 2 ''shЕҚ'' of vinegar, and 3 ''koku'' of ash, with 8 bundles of firewood as fuel. The result was a subdued yellow-brown or reddish-brown hue, rather than a bright sunlit tone. Variations occurred due to differences in craftsmanship and wearer specifications{{Sfn|Suzuki|1985}}. ### Patterns Early ''kЕҚrozen no hЕҚ'' designs featured all-over motifs (''sЕҚmon'') of ''paulownia,'' ''bamboo,'' and ''phoenix'' (''kiritake hЕҚЕҚ''){{Sfn|Suzuki|1983|p=254}}. The [[Heian period]] saw the introduction of rectangular designs (''hakogata mon''), which are preserved in the robes of later emperors and remain in modern usage. == Gallery == File:Emperor Showa.jpg|Emperor [[ShЕҚwa]] wearing the ''kЕҚrozen no hЕҚ'' during his enthronement ceremony in 1928. File:Ceremony of the Enthronement of His Majesty the Emperor at the Seiden7.jpg|''KЕҚrozen no hЕҚ'' worn by Emperor [[Naruhito]] during the 2019 enthronement ceremony. File:Rittaishi of Prince Naruhito.jpg|Emperor Naruhito (then Crown Prince) at his investiture. File:Emperor Takakura.jpg|Portrait of [[Emperor Takakura]] wearing the ''kЕҚrozen no hЕҚ,'' from the ''Tenshi Sekkan MyЕҚei.'' File:Tang Emperor Taizong.jpg|Portrait of [[Tang Taizong]], showing the Chinese tradition of yellow imperial robes. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kikujin no hЕҚ|ja|йәҙеЎөиўҚ}} * {{ill|ЕҢtan no hЕҚ|ja|й»„дё№иўҚ}} * {{ill|Mikado Yellow|ja|гғҹгӮ«гғүгӮӨгӮЁгғӯгғј}} == External Links == * [http://www.iz2.or.jp/fukushoku/f_disp.php?page_no=0000165 ''KЕҚrozen no hЕҚ'' replica], Costume Museum. {{Double image aside|right|Emperor Naruhito (cropped).jpg |209| Emperor Akihito 199011 1.jpg |214|Left: [[2019]] (Reiwa 1), Emperor [[Naruhito]] wearing the KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ
Right: [[1990]] (Heisei 2), Emperor [[Akihito]] wearing the same ceremonial robe during his reign.}} '''KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ''' (й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ) is a {{ill|sokutai|ja|жқҹеёҜ}} ceremonial robe worn exclusively by the [[Emperor of Japan]] during enthronement ceremonies and important rituals. It is a highly symbolic robe, dyed in the unique color known as {{ill|KЕҚrozen|ja|й»„ж«Ёжҹ“}}, reserved solely for the Emperor. The color kЕҚrozen is derived from natural dyes, specifically the bark of the {{ill|Yamahaze|ja|еұұж«Ё}} tree and the heartwood of {{ill|Sappanwood|ja|иҳҮиҠі}}. It has been described as a "reddish-yellow"{{Sfn|ж—Ҙжң¬еӣҪиӘһеӨ§иҫһе…ё|2000}} or "yellowish-brown."{{Sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}} Variations in tone are said to reflect differences in era and the wearer's age.{{Sfn|йҲҙжңЁ|1985}} In 2019, during the [[:en:2019_Japanese_imperial_transition#Enthronement_Ceremony|Sokutai Ceremony]], foreign media described the color as "brown-gold." [https://www.bbc.com/news/world-asia-50120563 'Naruhito: Japan's emperor proclaims enthronement in ancient ceremony'], BBC, October 22, 2019. == History and Origin == The origins of the KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ trace back to the [[Heian period]]. Initially formalized by Emperor [[Saga]] in {{ill|820|ja|ејҳд»Ғ11е№ҙ}}, the robe's use for specific court rituals and interactions was outlined in the imperial decree recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬зҙҖз•Ҙ}}. Before this, clothing regulations for emperors were not explicitly stated in earlier codes, such as the {{ill|YЕҚrЕҚ Code|ja|йӨҠиҖҒеҫӢд»Ө}}.{{Sfn|й»’еқӮ|1939|p=213}} KЕҚrozen's color was inspired by {{ill|Tang Dynasty|ja|е”җ}} culture but adapted uniquely in Japan. Unlike the {{ill|Zhehuangpao|zh|иөӯй»„иўҚ}} worn by Tang emperors, kЕҚrozen is associated with the radiant midday sun.{{sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}} This association is detailed in later writings, such as Takakura NagatsuneвҖҷs ''ZokushinsЕҚ HishЕҚ'' (1288), which states, "The color of the sun is befitting for the Emperor."{{sfn|жӯҰз”°|жҙҘз”°|2016|p=166}} Since the establishment of the robe's exclusive use for emperors, kЕҚrozen has been regarded as a {{ill|forbidden color|ja|зҰҒиүІ}}вҖ”strictly prohibited for use by anyone else.{{sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}} == Design and Construction == === Dyeing Process === According to the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸ}}, dyeing the kЕҚrozen fabric required a precise mixture of yamahaze bark and sappanwood. The result was a rich, subdued yellow-brown color, symbolizing the dignity of the Emperor. Due to the complexity of the process, achieving a consistent hue was challenging, even for skilled dyers.{{sfn|еҗүеІЎ|2000}} === Patterns and Textures === The robe's patterns have evolved. Initially, designs such as ''Kiritake HЕҚЕҚmon'' (paulownia, bamboo, and phoenix motifs) were used. By the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|е®Өз”әжҷӮд»Ј}}, the design transitioned to rectangular ''kakugata mon'' patterns incorporating mythical creatures like the phoenix and kirin.{{sfn|йҲҙжңЁ|1985}} These patterns remain in use today. === Fabric and Lining === Modern KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ robes are made with ''koteaya'' fabric, and their linings vary by tradition.{{sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}} == Ritual and Usage == The robe is integral to imperial rituals, including the {{ill|Enthronement of the Japanese emperor|ja|еҚідҪҚзӨјжӯЈж®ҝгҒ®е„Җ}}, {{ill|Shihohai|lt=Ritual of the Four Directions|ja|еӣӣж–№жӢқ}}, and other key {{ill|Imperial Household Shinto|ja|е®®дёӯзҘӯзҘҖ}} ceremonies. Exceptions include the {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|ж–°еҳ—зҘӯ}}, where the Emperor wears a ceremonial white robe (''gosai fuku''). == Gallery == File:Emperor Showa.jpg|[[1928]] (ShЕҚwa 3), Emperor [[ShЕҚwa]] wearing the KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ during his enthronement ceremony. File:Ceremony of the Enthronement of His Majesty the Emperor at the Seiden7.jpg|2019 (Reiwa 1), Emperor Naruhito during the ''Sokutai Ceremony.'' File:Rittaishi of Prince Naruhito.jpg|Crown Prince Naruhito's investiture ceremony. File:Emperor Takakura.jpg|Portrait of Emperor [[Takakura]], depicted in a KЕҚrozen robe. File:Tang Emperor Taizong.jpg|Tang Emperor Taizong wearing a robe similar in color to KЕҚrozen. == See Also == * {{ill|Kikujin robe|ja|йәҙеЎөиўҚ}} * {{ill|ЕҢni robe|ja|й»„дё№иўҚ}} * {{ill|Mikado yellow|ja|гғҹгӮ«гғүгӮӨгӮЁгғӯгғј|en}} == References == {{и„ҡжіЁгғҳгғ«гғ—}} {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.iz2.or.jp/fukushoku/f_disp.php?page_no=0000165 "KЕҚrozen Robes"], Costume revision.comment: Adding [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Double image aside|right|Emperor Naruhito (cropped).jpg |209| Emperor Akihito 199011 1.jpg |214|Left: Emperor [[Naruhito]] wearing the ''KЕҚrozen no hЕҚ'' (й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ) in the first year of the Reiwa era (2019).
Right: Emperor [[Akihito]] (at the time) wearing the ''KЕҚrozen no hЕҚ'' in 1990 (Heisei 2).}} '''KЕҚrozen no hЕҚ''' (й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ) is the ceremonial [[court dress]] of the [[Emperor of Japan]] since the [[Heian period]]. Worn during enthronement ceremonies and significant rituals, this ''hЕҚ'' (robe) is exclusively reserved for the Emperor and is dyed in the distinctive ''kЕҚrozen'' color. ''KЕҚrozen'' is a dye made from the bark of the [[wax tree]] and the heartwood of [[sappanwood]]. Its hue is described as a reddish-yellow{{Sfn|Nihon Kokugo Daijiten|2000}} or a yellowish-brown{{Sfn|Takada|1986}}. The shade varied depending on the time period and the wearer's age{{Sfn|Suzuki|1985}}. During the [[2019 enthronement ceremony]], foreign media described the color as {{lang|ja|brown-gold}}[https://www.bbc.com/news/world-asia-50120563 "Naruhito: Japan's emperor proclaims enthronement in ancient ceremony"], BBC, October 22, 2019.. == History and Origin == The ''[[YЕҚrЕҚ Code]]'' of the [[Nara period]] regulated [[formal dress]] for the [[Crown Prince]] and officials but did not specify the attire for the Emperor{{Sfn|Kurosaka|1939|p=213}}. The ''[[ShЕҚsЕҚin]] documents'' reveal that the Emperor's court attire (''kon-e'') included ''hakuno awasehЕҚ,'' a white silk robe{{Sfn|Imperial Household Museum|1929|p=65}}{{Sfn|KondЕҚ|2019|loc=В§ 1.2 (Kindle)}}. The ''kЕҚrozen no hЕҚ'' became established as imperial attire in the 11th year of the [[KЕҚnin era]] (820 CE). [[Emperor Saga]] decreed on the first day of the second month that ''kЕҚrozen'' robes be worn for specific occasions such as [[new moon]] ceremonies, [[listening to petitions]], receptions for foreign envoys, [[hЕҚbei]] rituals, and seasonal banquets. For [[Shinto]] rites or visits to [[imperial tombs]] in winter, ''haku-e'' (white robes) were designated; for [[New Year's]] celebrations, ''konmen-jЕ«nishЕҚ'' (еҚҒдәҢз« , twelve-embroidered robes) were used{{Sfn|Nihon Kiryaku|loc=KЕҚnin 11}}. These regulations reflected influences from [[Tang Dynasty]] [[China]], where yellow robes symbolized imperial authority. The ''kЕҚrozen'' hue was believed to represent the sun at its zenith, symbolizing imperial divinity{{Sfn|Takada|1986}}. == Design and Usage == ### Dyeing Process The ''[[Engishiki]]'' describes the materials for dyeing ''kЕҚrozen'' robes: 14 ''kin'' of wax tree bark, 11 ''kin'' of sappanwood, 2 ''shЕҚ'' of vinegar, and 3 ''koku'' of ash, with 8 bundles of firewood as fuel. The result was a subdued yellow-brown or reddish-brown hue, rather than a bright sunlit tone. Variations occurred due to differences in craftsmanship and wearer specifications{{Sfn|Suzuki|1985}}. ### Patterns Early ''kЕҚrozen no hЕҚ'' designs featured all-over motifs (''sЕҚmon'') of ''paulownia,'' ''bamboo,'' and ''phoenix'' (''kiritake hЕҚЕҚ''){{Sfn|Suzuki|1983|p=254}}. The [[Heian period]] saw the introduction of rectangular designs (''hakogata mon''), which are preserved in the robes of later emperors and remain in modern usage. == Gallery == File:Emperor Showa.jpg|Emperor [[ShЕҚwa]] wearing the ''kЕҚrozen no hЕҚ'' during his enthronement ceremony in 1928. File:Ceremony of the Enthronement of His Majesty the Emperor at the Seiden7.jpg|''KЕҚrozen no hЕҚ'' worn by Emperor [[Naruhito]] during the 2019 enthronement ceremony. File:Rittaishi of Prince Naruhito.jpg|Emperor Naruhito (then Crown Prince) at his investiture. File:Emperor Takakura.jpg|Portrait of [[Emperor Takakura]] wearing the ''kЕҚrozen no hЕҚ,'' from the ''Tenshi Sekkan MyЕҚei.'' File:Tang Emperor Taizong.jpg|Portrait of [[Tang Taizong]], showing the Chinese tradition of yellow imperial robes. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kikujin no hЕҚ|ja|йәҙеЎөиўҚ}} * {{ill|ЕҢtan no hЕҚ|ja|й»„дё№иўҚ}} * {{ill|Mikado Yellow|ja|гғҹгӮ«гғүгӮӨгӮЁгғӯгғј}} == External Links == * [http://www.iz2.or.jp/fukushoku/f_disp.php?page_no=0000165 ''KЕҚrozen no hЕҚ'' replica], Costume Museum. revision.comment: Adding [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[category{{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Double image aside|right|Emperor Naruhito (cropped).jpg |209| Emperor Akihito 199011 1.jpg |214|Left: [[2019]] (Reiwa 1), Emperor [[Naruhito]] wearing the KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ
Right: [[1990]] (Heisei 2), Emperor [[Akihito]] wearing the same ceremonial robe during his reign.}} '''KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ''' (й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ) is a {{ill|sokutai|ja|жқҹеёҜ}} ceremonial robe worn exclusively by the [[Emperor of Japan]] during enthronement ceremonies and important rituals. It is a highly symbolic robe, dyed in the unique color known as {{ill|KЕҚrozen|ja|й»„ж«Ёжҹ“}}, reserved solely for the Emperor. The color kЕҚrozen is derived from natural dyes, specifically the bark of the {{ill|Yamahaze|ja|еұұж«Ё}} tree and the heartwood of {{ill|Sappanwood|ja|иҳҮиҠі}}. It has been described as a "reddish-yellow"{{Sfn|ж—Ҙжң¬еӣҪиӘһеӨ§иҫһе…ё|2000}} or "yellowish-brown."{{Sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}} Variations in tone are said to reflect differences in era and the wearer's age.{{Sfn|йҲҙжңЁ|1985}} In 2019, during the [[:en:2019_Japanese_imperial_transition#Enthronement_Ceremony|Sokutai Ceremony]], foreign media described the color as "brown-gold." [https://www.bbc.com/news/world-asia-50120563 'Naruhito: Japan's emperor proclaims enthronement in ancient ceremony'], BBC, October 22, 2019. == History and Origin == The origins of the KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ trace back to the [[Heian period]]. Initially formalized by Emperor [[Saga]] in {{ill|820|ja|ејҳд»Ғ11е№ҙ}}, the robe's use for specific court rituals and interactions was outlined in the imperial decree recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬зҙҖз•Ҙ}}. Before this, clothing regulations for emperors were not explicitly stated in earlier codes, such as the {{ill|YЕҚrЕҚ Code|ja|йӨҠиҖҒеҫӢд»Ө}}.{{Sfn|й»’еқӮ|1939|p=213}} KЕҚrozen's color was inspired by {{ill|Tang Dynasty|ja|е”җ}} culture but adapted uniquely in Japan. Unlike the {{ill|Zhehuangpao|zh|иөӯй»„иўҚ}} worn by Tang emperors, kЕҚrozen is associated with the radiant midday sun.{{sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}} This association is detailed in later writings, such as Takakura NagatsuneвҖҷs ''ZokushinsЕҚ HishЕҚ'' (1288), which states, "The color of the sun is befitting for the Emperor."{{sfn|жӯҰз”°|жҙҘз”°|2016|p=166}} Since the establishment of the robe's exclusive use for emperors, kЕҚrozen has been regarded as a {{ill|forbidden color|ja|зҰҒиүІ}}вҖ”strictly prohibited for use by anyone else.{{sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}} == Design and Construction == === Dyeing Process === According to the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸ}}, dyeing the kЕҚrozen fabric required a precise mixture of yamahaze bark and sappanwood. The result was a rich, subdued yellow-brown color, symbolizing the dignity of the Emperor. Due to the complexity of the process, achieving a consistent hue was challenging, even for skilled dyers.{{sfn|еҗүеІЎ|2000}} === Patterns and Textures === The robe's patterns have evolved. Initially, designs such as ''Kiritake HЕҚЕҚmon'' (paulownia, bamboo, and phoenix motifs) were used. By the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|е®Өз”әжҷӮд»Ј}}, the design transitioned to rectangular ''kakugata mon'' patterns incorporating mythical creatures like the phoenix and kirin.{{sfn|йҲҙжңЁ|1985}} These patterns remain in use today. === Fabric and Lining === Modern KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ robes are made with ''koteaya'' fabric, and their linings vary by tradition.{{sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}} == Ritual and Usage == The robe is integral to imperial rituals, including the {{ill|Enthronement of the Japanese emperor|ja|еҚідҪҚзӨјжӯЈж®ҝгҒ®е„Җ}}, {{ill|Shihohai|lt=Ritual of the Four Directions|ja|еӣӣж–№жӢқ}}, and other key {{ill|Imperial Household Shinto|ja|е®®дёӯзҘӯзҘҖ}} ceremonies. Exceptions include the {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|ж–°еҳ—зҘӯ}}, where the Emperor wears a ceremonial white robe (''gosai fuku''). == Gallery == File:Emperor Showa.jpg|[[1928]] (ShЕҚwa 3), Emperor [[ShЕҚwa]] wearing the KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ during his enthronement ceremony. File:Ceremony of the Enthronement of His Majesty the Emperor at the Seiden7.jpg|2019 (Reiwa 1), Emperor Naruhito during the ''Sokutai Ceremony.'' File:Rittaishi of Prince Naruhito.jpg|Crown Prince Naruhito's investiture ceremony. File:Emperor Takakura.jpg|Portrait of Emperor [[Takakura]], depicted in a KЕҚrozen robe. File:Tang Emperor Taizong.jpg|Tang Emperor Taizong wearing a robe similar in color to KЕҚrozen. == See Also == * {{ill|Kikujin robe|ja|йәҙеЎөиўҚ}} * {{ill|ЕҢni robe|ja|й»„дё№иўҚ}} * {{ill|Mikado yellow|ja|гғҹгӮ«гғүгӮӨгӮЁгғӯгғј|en}} == References == {{и„ҡжіЁгғҳгғ«гғ—}} {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.iz2.or.jp/fukushoku/f_disp.php?page_no=0000165 "KЕҚrozen Robes"], Costume Museum (replica of modern KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ). {{DEFAULTSORT:KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Kourozen-no-gohou]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Kourozen-no-gohou]][[Category:Pages needing The shade varied depending on the time period and the wearer's age{{Sfn|Suzuki|1985}}. During the [[2019 enthronement ceremony]], foreign media described the color as {{lang|ja|brown-gold}}[https://www.bbc.com/news/world-asia-50120563 "Naruhito: Japan's emperor proclaims enthronement in ancient ceremony"], BBC, October 22, 2019.. == History and Origin == The ''[[YЕҚrЕҚ Code]]'' of the [[Nara period]] regulated [[formal dress]] for the [[Crown Prince]] and officials but did not specify the attire for the Emperor{{Sfn|Kurosaka|1939|p=213}}. The ''[[ShЕҚsЕҚin]] documents'' reveal that the Emperor's court attire (''kon-e'') included ''hakuno awasehЕҚ,'' a white silk robe{{Sfn|Imperial Household Museum|1929|p=65}}{{Sfn|KondЕҚ|2019|loc=В§ 1.2 (Kindle)}}. The ''kЕҚrozen no hЕҚ'' became established as imperial attire in the 11th year of the [[KЕҚnin era]] (820 CE). [[Emperor Saga]] decreed on the first day of the second month that ''kЕҚrozen'' robes be worn for specific occasions such as [[new moon]] ceremonies, [[listening to petitions]], receptions for foreign envoys, [[hЕҚbei]] rituals, and seasonal banquets. For [[Shinto]] rites or visits to [[imperial tombs]] in winter, ''haku-e'' (white robes) were designated; for [[New Year's]] celebrations, ''konmen-jЕ«nishЕҚ'' (еҚҒдәҢз« , twelve-embroidered robes) were used{{Sfn|Nihon Kiryaku|loc=KЕҚnin 11}}. These regulations reflected influences from [[Tang Dynasty]] [[China]], where yellow robes symbolized imperial authority. The ''kЕҚrozen'' hue was believed to represent the sun at its zenith, symbolizing imperial divinity{{Sfn|Takada|1986}}. == Design and Usage == ### Dyeing Process The ''[[Engishiki]]'' describes the materials for dyeing ''kЕҚrozen'' robes: 14 ''kin'' of wax tree bark, 11 ''kin'' of sappanwood, 2 ''shЕҚ'' of vinegar, and 3 ''koku'' of ash, with 8 bundles of firewood as fuel. The result was a subdued yellow-brown or reddish-brown hue, rather than a bright sunlit tone. Variations occurred due to differences in craftsmanship and wearer specifications{{Sfn|Suzuki|1985}}. ### Patterns Early ''kЕҚrozen no hЕҚ'' designs featured all-over motifs (''sЕҚmon'') of ''paulownia,'' ''bamboo,'' and ''phoenix'' (''kiritake hЕҚЕҚ''){{Sfn|Suzuki|1983|p=254}}. The [[Heian period]] saw the introduction of rectangular designs (''hakogata mon''), which are preserved in the robes of later emperors and remain in modern usage. == Gallery == File:Emperor Showa.jpg|Emperor [[ShЕҚwa]] wearing the ''kЕҚrozen no hЕҚ'' during his enthronement ceremony in 1928. File:Ceremony of the Enthronement of His Majesty the Emperor at the Seiden7.jpg|''KЕҚrozen no hЕҚ'' worn by Emperor [[Naruhito]] during the 2019 enthronement ceremony. File:Rittaishi of Prince Naruhito.jpg|Emperor Naruhito (then Crown Prince) at his investiture. File:Emperor Takakura.jpg|Portrait of [[Emperor Takakura]] wearing the ''kЕҚrozen no hЕҚ,'' from the ''Tenshi Sekkan MyЕҚei.'' File:Tang Emperor Taizong.jpg|Portrait of [[Tang Taizong]], showing the Chinese tradition of yellow imperial robes. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kikujin no hЕҚ|ja|йәҙеЎөиўҚ}} * {{ill|ЕҢtan no hЕҚ|ja|й»„дё№иўҚ}} * {{ill|Mikado Yellow|ja|гғҹгӮ«гғүгӮӨгӮЁгғӯгғј}} == External Links == * [http://www.iz2.or.jp/fukushoku/f_disp.php?page_no=0000165 ''KЕҚrozen no hЕҚ'' replica], Costume Museum. {{Double image aside|right|Emperor Naruhito (cropped).jpg |209| Emperor Akihito 199011 1.jpg |214|Left: [[2019]] (Reiwa 1), Emperor [[Naruhito]] wearing the KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ
Right: [[1990]] (Heisei 2), Emperor [[Akihito]] wearing the same ceremonial robe during his reign.}} '''KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ''' (й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ) is a {{ill|sokutai|ja|жқҹеёҜ}} ceremonial robe worn exclusively by the [[Emperor of Japan]] during enthronement ceremonies and important rituals. It is a highly symbolic robe, dyed in the unique color known as {{ill|KЕҚrozen|ja|й»„ж«Ёжҹ“}}, reserved solely for the Emperor. The color kЕҚrozen is derived from natural dyes, specifically the bark of the {{ill|Yamahaze|ja|еұұж«Ё}} tree and the heartwood of {{ill|Sappanwood|ja|иҳҮиҠі}}. It has been described as a "reddish-yellow"{{Sfn|ж—Ҙжң¬еӣҪиӘһеӨ§иҫһе…ё|2000}} or "yellowish-brown."{{Sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}} Variations in tone are said to reflect differences in era and the wearer's age.{{Sfn|йҲҙжңЁ|1985}} In 2019, during the [[:en:2019_Japanese_imperial_transition#Enthronement_Ceremony|Sokutai Ceremony]], foreign media described the color as "brown-gold." [https://www.bbc.com/news/world-asia-50120563 'Naruhito: Japan's emperor proclaims enthronement in ancient ceremony'], BBC, October 22, 2019. == History and Origin == The origins of the KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ trace back to the [[Heian period]]. Initially formalized by Emperor [[Saga]] in {{ill|820|ja|ејҳд»Ғ11е№ҙ}}, the robe's use for specific court rituals and interactions was outlined in the imperial decree recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬зҙҖз•Ҙ}}. Before this, clothing regulations for emperors were not explicitly stated in earlier codes, such as the {{ill|YЕҚrЕҚ Code|ja|йӨҠиҖҒеҫӢд»Ө}}.{{Sfn|й»’еқӮ|1939|p=213}} KЕҚrozen's color was inspired by {{ill|Tang Dynasty|ja|е”җ}} culture but adapted uniquely in Japan. Unlike the {{ill|Zhehuangpao|zh|иөӯй»„иўҚ}} worn by Tang emperors, kЕҚrozen is associated with the radiant midday sun.{{sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}} This association is detailed in later writings, such as Takakura NagatsuneвҖҷs ''ZokushinsЕҚ HishЕҚ'' (1288), which states, "The color of the sun is befitting for the Emperor."{{sfn|жӯҰз”°|жҙҘз”°|2016|p=166}} Since the establishment of the robe's exclusive use for emperors, kЕҚrozen has been regarded as a {{ill|forbidden color|ja|зҰҒиүІ}}вҖ”strictly prohibited for use by anyone else.{{sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}} == Design and Construction == === Dyeing Process === According to the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸ}}, dyeing the kЕҚrozen fabric required a precise mixture of yamahaze bark and sappanwood. The result was a rich, subdued yellow-brown color, symbolizing the dignity of the Emperor. Due to the complexity of the process, achieving a consistent hue was challenging, even for skilled dyers.{{sfn|еҗүеІЎ|2000}} === Patterns and Textures === The robe's patterns have evolved. Initially, designs such as ''Kiritake HЕҚЕҚmon'' (paulownia, bamboo, and phoenix motifs) were used. By the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|е®Өз”әжҷӮд»Ј}}, the design transitioned to rectangular ''kakugata mon'' patterns incorporating mythical creatures like the phoenix and kirin.{{sfn|йҲҙжңЁ|1985}} These patterns remain in use today. === Fabric and Lining === Modern KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ robes are made with ''koteaya'' fabric, and their linings vary by tradition.{{sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}} == Ritual and Usage == The robe is integral to imperial rituals, including the {{ill|Enthronement of the Japanese emperor|ja|еҚідҪҚзӨјжӯЈж®ҝгҒ®е„Җ}}, {{ill|Shihohai|lt=Ritual of the Four Directions|ja|еӣӣж–№жӢқ}}, and other key {{ill|Imperial Household Shinto|ja|е®®дёӯзҘӯзҘҖ}} ceremonies. Exceptions include the {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|ж–°еҳ—зҘӯ}}, where the Emperor wears a ceremonial white robe (''gosai fuku''). == Gallery == File:Emperor Showa.jpg|[[1928]] (ShЕҚwa 3), Emperor [[ShЕҚwa]] wearing the KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ during his enthronement ceremony. File:Ceremony of the Enthronement of His Majesty the Emperor at the Seiden7.jpg|2019 (Reiwa 1), Emperor Naruhito during the ''Sokutai Ceremony.'' File:Rittaishi of Prince Naruhito.jpg|Crown Prince Naruhito's investiture ceremony. File:Emperor Takakura.jpg|Portrait of Emperor [[Takakura]], depicted in a KЕҚrozen robe. File:Tang Emperor Taizong.jpg|Tang Emperor Taizong wearing a robe similar in color to KЕҚrozen. == See Also == * {{ill|Kikujin robe|ja|йәҙеЎөиўҚ}} * {{ill|ЕҢni robe|ja|й»„дё№иўҚ}} * {{ill|Mikado yellow|ja|гғҹгӮ«гғүгӮӨгӮЁгғӯгғј|en}} == References == {{и„ҡжіЁгғҳгғ«гғ—}} {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.iz2.or.jp/fukushoku/f_disp.php?page_no=0000165 "KЕҚrozen Robes"], Costume {{Double image aside|right|Emperor Naruhito (cropped).jpg |209| Emperor Akihito 199011 1.jpg |214|Left: Emperor [[Naruhito]] wearing the ''KЕҚrozen no hЕҚ'' (й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ) in the first year of the Reiwa era (2019).
Right: Emperor [[Akihito]] (at the time) wearing the ''KЕҚrozen no hЕҚ'' in 1990 (Heisei 2).}} '''KЕҚrozen no hЕҚ''' (й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ) is the ceremonial [[court dress]] of the [[Emperor of Japan]] since the [[Heian period]]. Worn during enthronement ceremonies and significant rituals, this ''hЕҚ'' (robe) is exclusively reserved for the Emperor and is dyed in the distinctive ''kЕҚrozen'' color. ''KЕҚrozen'' is a dye made from the bark of the [[wax tree]] and the heartwood of [[sappanwood]]. Its hue is described as a reddish-yellow{{Sfn|Nihon Kokugo Daijiten|2000}} or a yellowish-brown{{Sfn|Takada|1986}}. The shade varied depending on the time period and the wearer's age{{Sfn|Suzuki|1985}}. During the [[2019 enthronement ceremony]], foreign media described the color as {{lang|ja|brown-gold}}[https://www.bbc.com/news/world-asia-50120563 "Naruhito: Japan's emperor proclaims enthronement in ancient ceremony"], BBC, October 22, 2019.. == History and Origin == The ''[[YЕҚrЕҚ Code]]'' of the [[Nara period]] regulated [[formal dress]] for the [[Crown Prince]] and officials but did not specify the attire for the Emperor{{Sfn|Kurosaka|1939|p=213}}. The ''[[ShЕҚsЕҚin]] documents'' reveal that the Emperor's court attire (''kon-e'') included ''hakuno awasehЕҚ,'' a white silk robe{{Sfn|Imperial Household Museum|1929|p=65}}{{Sfn|KondЕҚ|2019|loc=В§ 1.2 (Kindle)}}. The ''kЕҚrozen no hЕҚ'' became established as imperial attire in the 11th year of the [[KЕҚnin era]] (820 CE). [[Emperor Saga]] decreed on the first day of the second month that ''kЕҚrozen'' robes be worn for specific occasions such as [[new moon]] ceremonies, [[listening to petitions]], receptions for foreign envoys, [[hЕҚbei]] rituals, and seasonal banquets. For [[Shinto]] rites or visits to [[imperial tombs]] in winter, ''haku-e'' (white robes) were designated; for [[New Year's]] celebrations, ''konmen-jЕ«nishЕҚ'' (еҚҒдәҢз« , twelve-embroidered robes) were used{{Sfn|Nihon Kiryaku|loc=KЕҚnin 11}}. These regulations reflected influences from [[Tang Dynasty]] [[China]], where yellow robes symbolized imperial authority. The ''kЕҚrozen'' hue was believed to represent the sun at its zenith, symbolizing imperial divinity{{Sfn|Takada|1986}}. == Design and Usage == ### Dyeing Process The ''[[Engishiki]]'' describes the materials for dyeing ''kЕҚrozen'' robes: 14 ''kin'' of wax tree bark, 11 ''kin'' of sappanwood, 2 ''shЕҚ'' of vinegar, and 3 ''koku'' of ash, with 8 bundles of firewood as fuel. The result was a subdued yellow-brown or reddish-brown hue, rather than a bright sunlit tone. Variations occurred due to differences in craftsmanship and wearer specifications{{Sfn|Suzuki|1985}}. ### Patterns Early ''kЕҚrozen no hЕҚ'' designs featured all-over motifs (''sЕҚmon'') of ''paulownia,'' ''bamboo,'' and ''phoenix'' (''kiritake hЕҚЕҚ''){{Sfn|Suzuki|1983|p=254}}. The [[Heian period]] saw the introduction of rectangular designs (''hakogata mon''), which are preserved in the robes of later emperors and remain in modern usage. == Gallery == File:Emperor Showa.jpg|Emperor [[ShЕҚwa]] wearing the ''kЕҚrozen no hЕҚ'' during his enthronement ceremony in 1928. File:Ceremony of the Enthronement of His Majesty the Emperor at the Seiden7.jpg|''KЕҚrozen no hЕҚ'' worn by Emperor [[Naruhito]] during the 2019 enthronement ceremony. File:Rittaishi of Prince Naruhito.jpg|Emperor Naruhito (then Crown Prince) at his investiture. File:Emperor Takakura.jpg|Portrait of [[Emperor Takakura]] wearing the ''kЕҚrozen no hЕҚ,'' from the ''Tenshi Sekkan MyЕҚei.'' File:Tang Emperor Taizong.jpg|Portrait of [[Tang Taizong]], showing the Chinese tradition of yellow imperial robes. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kikujin no hЕҚ|ja|йәҙеЎөиўҚ}} * {{ill|ЕҢtan no hЕҚ|ja|й»„дё№иўҚ}} * {{ill|Mikado Yellow|ja|гғҹгӮ«гғүгӮӨгӮЁгғӯгғј}} == External Links == * [http://www.iz2.or.jp/fukushoku/f_disp.php?page_no=0000165 ''KЕҚrozen no hЕҚ'' replica], Costume Museum. Right: [[1990]] (Heisei 2), Emperor [[Akihito]] wearing the same ceremonial robe during his reign.}} '''KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ''' (й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ) is a {{ill|sokutai|ja|жқҹеёҜ}} ceremonial robe worn exclusively by the [[Emperor of Japan]] during enthronement ceremonies and important rituals. It is a highly symbolic robe, dyed in the unique color known as {{ill|KЕҚrozen|ja|й»„ж«Ёжҹ“}}, reserved solely for the Emperor. The color kЕҚrozen is derived from natural dyes, specifically the bark of the {{ill|Yamahaze|ja|еұұж«Ё}} tree and the heartwood of {{ill|Sappanwood|ja|иҳҮиҠі}}. It has been described as a "reddish-yellow"{{Sfn|ж—Ҙжң¬еӣҪиӘһеӨ§иҫһе…ё|2000}} or "yellowish-brown."{{Sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}} Variations in tone are said to reflect differences in era and the wearer's age.{{Sfn|йҲҙжңЁ|1985}} In 2019, during the [[:en:2019_Japanese_imperial_transition#Enthronement_Ceremony|Sokutai Ceremony]], foreign media described the color as "brown-gold." [https://www.bbc.com/news/world-asia-50120563 'Naruhito: Japan's emperor proclaims enthronement in ancient ceremony'], BBC, October 22, 2019. == History and Origin == The origins of the KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ trace back to the [[Heian period]]. Initially formalized by Emperor [[Saga]] in {{ill|820|ja|ејҳд»Ғ11е№ҙ}}, the robe's use for specific court rituals and interactions was outlined in the imperial decree recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬зҙҖз•Ҙ}}. Before this, clothing regulations for emperors were not explicitly stated in earlier codes, such as the {{ill|YЕҚrЕҚ Code|ja|йӨҠиҖҒеҫӢд»Ө}}.{{Sfn|й»’еқӮ|1939|p=213}} KЕҚrozen's color was inspired by {{ill|Tang Dynasty|ja|е”җ}} culture but adapted uniquely in Japan. Unlike the {{ill|Zhehuangpao|zh|иөӯй»„иўҚ}} worn by Tang emperors, kЕҚrozen is associated with the radiant midday sun.{{sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}} This association is detailed in later writings, such as Takakura NagatsuneвҖҷs ''ZokushinsЕҚ HishЕҚ'' (1288), which states, "The color of the sun is befitting for the Emperor."{{sfn|жӯҰз”°|жҙҘз”°|2016|p=166}} Since the establishment of the robe's exclusive use for emperors, kЕҚrozen has been regarded as a {{ill|forbidden color|ja|зҰҒиүІ}}вҖ”strictly prohibited for use by anyone else.{{sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}} == Design and Construction == === Dyeing Process === According to the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸ}}, dyeing the kЕҚrozen fabric required a precise mixture of yamahaze bark and sappanwood. The result was a rich, subdued yellow-brown color, symbolizing the dignity of the Emperor. Due to the complexity of the process, achieving a consistent hue was challenging, even for skilled dyers.{{sfn|еҗүеІЎ|2000}} === Patterns and Textures === The robe's patterns have evolved. Initially, designs such as ''Kiritake HЕҚЕҚmon'' (paulownia, bamboo, and phoenix motifs) were used. By the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|е®Өз”әжҷӮд»Ј}}, the design transitioned to rectangular ''kakugata mon'' patterns incorporating mythical creatures like the phoenix and kirin.{{sfn|йҲҙжңЁ|1985}} These patterns remain in use today. === Fabric and Lining === Modern KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ robes are made with ''koteaya'' fabric, and their linings vary by tradition.{{sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}} == Ritual and Usage == The robe is integral to imperial rituals, including the {{ill|Enthronement of the Japanese emperor|ja|еҚідҪҚзӨјжӯЈж®ҝгҒ®е„Җ}}, {{ill|Shihohai|lt=Ritual of the Four Directions|ja|еӣӣж–№жӢқ}}, and other key {{ill|Imperial Household Shinto|ja|е®®дёӯзҘӯзҘҖ}} ceremonies. Exceptions include the {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|ж–°еҳ—зҘӯ}}, where the Emperor wears a ceremonial white robe (''gosai fuku''). == Gallery == File:Emperor Showa.jpg|[[1928]] (ShЕҚwa 3), Emperor [[ShЕҚwa]] wearing the KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ during his enthronement ceremony. File:Ceremony of the Enthronement of His Majesty the Emperor at the Seiden7.jpg|2019 (Reiwa 1), Emperor Naruhito during the ''Sokutai Ceremony.'' File:Rittaishi of Prince Naruhito.jpg|Crown Prince Naruhito's investiture ceremony. File:Emperor Takakura.jpg|Portrait of Emperor [[Takakura]], depicted in a KЕҚrozen robe. File:Tang Emperor Taizong.jpg|Tang Emperor Taizong wearing a robe similar in color to KЕҚrozen. == See Also == * {{ill|Kikujin robe|ja|йәҙеЎөиўҚ}} * {{ill|ЕҢni robe|ja|й»„дё№иўҚ}} * {{ill|Mikado {{Double image aside|right|Emperor Naruhito (cropped).jpg |209| Emperor Akihito 199011 1.jpg |214|Left: Emperor [[Naruhito]] wearing the ''KЕҚrozen no hЕҚ'' (й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ) in the first year of the Reiwa era (2019).
Right: Emperor [[Akihito]] (at the time) wearing the ''KЕҚrozen no hЕҚ'' in 1990 (Heisei 2).}} '''KЕҚrozen no hЕҚ''' (й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ) is the ceremonial [[court dress]] of the [[Emperor of Japan]] since the [[Heian period]]. Worn during enthronement ceremonies and significant rituals, this ''hЕҚ'' (robe) is exclusively reserved for the Emperor and is dyed in the distinctive ''kЕҚrozen'' color. ''KЕҚrozen'' is a dye made from the bark of the [[wax tree]] and the heartwood of [[sappanwood]]. Its hue is described as a reddish-yellow{{Sfn|Nihon Kokugo Daijiten|2000}} or a yellowish-brown{{Sfn|Takada|1986}}. The shade varied depending on the time period and the wearer's age{{Sfn|Suzuki|1985}}. During the [[2019 enthronement ceremony]], foreign media described the color as {{lang|ja|brown-gold}}[https://www.bbc.com/news/world-asia-50120563 "Naruhito: Japan's emperor proclaims enthronement in ancient ceremony"], BBC, October 22, 2019.. == History and Origin == The ''[[YЕҚrЕҚ Code]]'' of the [[Nara period]] regulated [[formal dress]] for the [[Crown Prince]] and officials but did not specify the attire for the Emperor{{Sfn|Kurosaka|1939|p=213}}. The ''[[ShЕҚsЕҚin]] documents'' reveal that the Emperor's court attire (''kon-e'') included ''hakuno awasehЕҚ,'' a white silk robe{{Sfn|Imperial Household Museum|1929|p=65}}{{Sfn|KondЕҚ|2019|loc=В§ 1.2 (Kindle)}}. The ''kЕҚrozen no hЕҚ'' became established as imperial attire in the 11th year of the [[KЕҚnin era]] (820 CE). [[Emperor Saga]] decreed on the first day of the second month that ''kЕҚrozen'' robes be worn for specific occasions such as [[new moon]] ceremonies, [[listening to petitions]], receptions for foreign envoys, [[hЕҚbei]] rituals, and seasonal banquets. For [[Shinto]] rites or visits to [[Japanese imperial tombs|imperial tombs]] in winter, ''haku-e'' (white robes) were designated; for [[New Year's]] celebrations, ''konmen-jЕ«nishЕҚ'' (еҚҒдәҢз« , twelve-embroidered robes) were used{{Sfn|Nihon Kiryaku|loc=KЕҚnin 11}}. These regulations reflected influences from [[Tang Dynasty]] [[China]], where yellow robes symbolized imperial authority. The ''kЕҚrozen'' hue was believed to represent the sun at its zenith, symbolizing imperial divinity{{Sfn|Takada|1986}}. == Design and Usage == ### Dyeing Process The ''[[Engishiki]]'' describes the materials for dyeing ''kЕҚrozen'' robes: 14 ''kin'' of wax tree bark, 11 ''kin'' of sappanwood, 2 ''shЕҚ'' of vinegar, and 3 ''koku'' of ash, with 8 bundles of firewood as fuel. The result was a subdued yellow-brown or reddish-brown hue, rather than a bright sunlit tone. Variations occurred due to differences in craftsmanship and wearer specifications{{Sfn|Suzuki|1985}}. ### Patterns Early ''kЕҚrozen no hЕҚ'' designs featured all-over motifs (''sЕҚmon'') of ''paulownia,'' ''bamboo,'' and ''phoenix'' (''kiritake hЕҚЕҚ''){{Sfn|Suzuki|1983|p=254}}. The [[Heian period]] saw the introduction of rectangular designs (''hakogata mon''), which are preserved in the robes of later emperors and remain in modern usage. == Gallery == File:Emperor Showa.jpg|Emperor [[ShЕҚwa]] wearing the ''kЕҚrozen no hЕҚ'' during his enthronement ceremony in 1928. File:Ceremony of the Enthronement of His Majesty the Emperor at the Seiden7.jpg|''KЕҚrozen no hЕҚ'' worn by Emperor [[Naruhito]] during the 2019 enthronement ceremony. File:Rittaishi of Prince Naruhito.jpg|Emperor Naruhito (then Crown Prince) at his investiture. File:Emperor Takakura.jpg|Portrait of [[Emperor Takakura]] wearing the ''kЕҚrozen no hЕҚ,'' from the ''Tenshi Sekkan MyЕҚei.'' File:Tang Emperor Taizong.jpg|Portrait of [[Tang Taizong]], showing the Chinese tradition of yellow imperial robes. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kikujin no hЕҚ|ja|йәҙеЎөиўҚ}} * {{ill|ЕҢtan no hЕҚ|ja|й»„дё№иўҚ}} * {{ill|Mikado Yellow|ja|гғҹгӮ«гғүгӮӨгӮЁгғӯгғј}} == External Links == * [http://www.iz2.or.jp/fukushoku/f_disp.php?page_no=0000165 ''KЕҚrozen no hЕҚ'' replica], Costume Museum. {{Double image aside|right|Emperor Naruhito (cropped).jpg |209| Emperor Akihito 199011 1.jpg |214|Left: [[2019]] (Reiwa 1), Emperor [[Naruhito]] wearing the KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ
Right: [[1990]] (Heisei 2), Emperor [[Akihito]] wearing the same ceremonial robe during his reign.}} '''KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ''' (й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ) is a {{ill|sokutai|ja|жқҹеёҜ}} ceremonial robe worn exclusively by the [[Emperor of Japan]] during enthronement ceremonies and important rituals. It is a highly symbolic robe, dyed in the unique color known as {{ill|KЕҚrozen|ja|й»„ж«Ёжҹ“}}, reserved solely for the Emperor. The color kЕҚrozen is derived from natural dyes, specifically the bark of the {{ill|Yamahaze|ja|еұұж«Ё}} tree and the heartwood of {{ill|Sappanwood|ja|иҳҮиҠі}}. It has been described as a "reddish-yellow"{{Sfn|ж—Ҙжң¬еӣҪиӘһеӨ§иҫһе…ё|2000}} or "yellowish-brown."{{Sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}} Variations in tone are said to reflect differences in era and the wearer's age.{{Sfn|йҲҙжңЁ|1985}} In 2019, during the [[:en:2019_Japanese_imperial_transition#Enthronement_Ceremony|Sokutai Ceremony]], foreign media described the color as "brown-gold." [https://www.bbc.com/news/world-asia-50120563 'Naruhito: Japan's emperor proclaims enthronement in ancient ceremony'], BBC, October 22, 2019. == History and Origin == The origins of the KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ trace back to the [[Heian period]]. Initially formalized by Emperor [[Saga]] in {{ill|820|ja|ејҳд»Ғ11е№ҙ}}, the robe's use for specific court rituals and interactions was outlined in the imperial decree recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬зҙҖз•Ҙ}}. Before this, clothing regulations for emperors were not explicitly stated in earlier codes, such as the {{ill|YЕҚrЕҚ Code|ja|йӨҠиҖҒеҫӢд»Ө}}.{{Sfn|й»’еқӮ|1939|p=213}} KЕҚrozen's color was inspired by {{ill|Tang Dynasty|ja|е”җ}} culture but adapted uniquely in Japan. Unlike the {{ill|Zhehuangpao|zh|иөӯй»„иўҚ}} worn by Tang emperors, kЕҚrozen is associated with the radiant midday sun.{{sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}} This association is detailed in later writings, such as Takakura NagatsuneвҖҷs ''ZokushinsЕҚ HishЕҚ'' (1288), which states, "The color of the sun is befitting for the Emperor."{{sfn|жӯҰз”°|жҙҘз”°|2016|p=166}} Since the establishment of the robe's exclusive use for emperors, kЕҚrozen has been regarded as a {{ill|forbidden color|ja|зҰҒиүІ}}вҖ”strictly prohibited for use by anyone else.{{sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}} == Design and Construction == === Dyeing Process === According to the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸ}}, dyeing the kЕҚrozen fabric required a precise mixture of yamahaze bark and sappanwood. The result was a rich, subdued yellow-brown color, symbolizing the dignity of the Emperor. Due to the complexity of the process, achieving a consistent hue was challenging, even for skilled dyers.{{sfn|еҗүеІЎ|2000}} === Patterns and Textures === The robe's patterns have evolved. Initially, designs such as ''Kiritake HЕҚЕҚmon'' (paulownia, bamboo, and phoenix motifs) were used. By the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|е®Өз”әжҷӮд»Ј}}, the design transitioned to rectangular ''kakugata mon'' patterns incorporating mythical creatures like the phoenix and kirin.{{sfn|йҲҙжңЁ|1985}} These patterns remain in use today. === Fabric and Lining === Modern KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ robes are made with ''koteaya'' fabric, and their linings vary by tradition.{{sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}} == Ritual and Usage == The robe is integral to imperial rituals, including the {{ill|Enthronement of the Japanese emperor|ja|еҚідҪҚзӨјжӯЈж®ҝгҒ®е„Җ}}, {{ill|Shihohai|lt=Ritual of the Four Directions|ja|еӣӣж–№жӢқ}}, and other key {{ill|Imperial Household Shinto|ja|е®®дёӯзҘӯзҘҖ}} ceremonies. Exceptions include the {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|ж–°еҳ—зҘӯ}}, where the Emperor wears a ceremonial white robe (''gosai fuku''). == Gallery == File:Emperor Showa.jpg|[[1928]] (ShЕҚwa 3), Emperor [[ShЕҚwa]] wearing the KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ during his enthronement ceremony. File:Ceremony of the Enthronement of His Majesty the Emperor at the Seiden7.jpg|2019 (Reiwa 1), Emperor Naruhito during the ''Sokutai Ceremony.'' File:Rittaishi of Prince Naruhito.jpg|Crown Prince Naruhito's investiture ceremony. File:Emperor Takakura.jpg|Portrait of Emperor [[Takakura]], depicted in a KЕҚrozen robe. File:Tang Emperor Taizong.jpg|Tang Emperor Taizong wearing a robe similar in color to KЕҚrozen. == See Also == * {{ill|Kikujin robe|ja|йәҙеЎөиўҚ}} * {{ill|ЕҢni robe|ja|й»„дё№иўҚ}} * {{ill|Mikado {{Double image aside|right|Emperor Naruhito (cropped).jpg |209| Emperor Akihito 199011 1.jpg |214|Left: Emperor [[Naruhito]] wearing the ''KЕҚrozen no hЕҚ'' (й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ) in the first year of the Reiwa era (2019).
Right: Emperor [[Akihito]] (at the time) wearing the ''KЕҚrozen no hЕҚ'' in 1990 (Heisei 2).}} '''KЕҚrozen no hЕҚ''' (й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ) is the ceremonial [[court dress]] of the [[Emperor of Japan]] since the [[Heian period]]. Worn during enthronement ceremonies and significant rituals, this ''hЕҚ'' (robe) is exclusively reserved for the Emperor and is dyed in the distinctive ''kЕҚrozen'' color. ''KЕҚrozen'' is a dye made from the bark of the [[wax tree]] and the heartwood of [[sappanwood]]. Its hue is described as a reddish-yellow{{Sfn|Nihon Kokugo Daijiten|2000}} or a yellowish-brown{{Sfn|Takada|1986}}. The shade varied depending on the time period and the wearer's age{{Sfn|Suzuki|1985}}. During the [[2019 enthronement ceremony]], foreign media described the color as {{lang|ja|brown-gold}}[https://www.bbc.com/news/world-asia-50120563 "Naruhito: Japan's emperor proclaims enthronement in ancient ceremony"], BBC, October 22, 2019.. == History and Origin == The ''[[YЕҚrЕҚ Code]]'' of the [[Nara period]] regulated [[formal dress]] for the [[Crown Prince]] and officials but did not specify the attire for the Emperor{{Sfn|Kurosaka|1939|p=213}}. The ''[[ShЕҚsЕҚin]] documents'' reveal that the Emperor's court attire (''kon-e'') included ''hakuno awasehЕҚ,'' a white silk robe{{Sfn|Imperial Household Museum|1929|p=65}}{{Sfn|KondЕҚ|2019|loc=В§ 1.2 (Kindle)}}. The ''kЕҚrozen no hЕҚ'' became established as imperial attire in the 11th year of the [[KЕҚnin era]] (820 CE). [[Emperor Saga]] decreed on the first day of the second month that ''kЕҚrozen'' robes be worn for specific occasions such as [[new moon]] ceremonies, [[listening to petitions]], receptions for foreign envoys, [[:ja:еҘүе№Ј|hЕҚbei]] rituals, and seasonal banquets. For [[Shinto]] rites or visits to [[Japanese imperial tombs|imperial tombs]] in winter, ''haku-e'' (white robes) were designated; for [[New Year's]] celebrations, ''konmen-jЕ«nishЕҚ'' (еҚҒдәҢз« , twelve-embroidered robes) were used{{Sfn|Nihon Kiryaku|loc=KЕҚnin 11}}. These regulations reflected influences from [[Tang Dynasty]] [[China]], where yellow robes symbolized imperial authority. The ''kЕҚrozen'' hue was believed to represent the sun at its zenith, symbolizing imperial divinity{{Sfn|Takada|1986}}. == Design and Usage == ### Dyeing Process The ''[[Engishiki]]'' describes the materials for dyeing ''kЕҚrozen'' robes: 14 ''kin'' of wax tree bark, 11 ''kin'' of sappanwood, 2 ''shЕҚ'' of vinegar, and 3 ''koku'' of ash, with 8 bundles of firewood as fuel. The result was a subdued yellow-brown or reddish-brown hue, rather than a bright sunlit tone. Variations occurred due to differences in craftsmanship and wearer specifications{{Sfn|Suzuki|1985}}. ### Patterns Early ''kЕҚrozen no hЕҚ'' designs featured all-over motifs (''sЕҚmon'') of ''paulownia,'' ''bamboo,'' and ''phoenix'' (''kiritake hЕҚЕҚ''){{Sfn|Suzuki|1983|p=254}}. The [[Heian period]] saw the introduction of rectangular designs (''hakogata mon''), which are preserved in the robes of later emperors and remain in modern usage. == Gallery == File:Emperor Showa.jpg|Emperor [[ShЕҚwa]] wearing the ''kЕҚrozen no hЕҚ'' during his enthronement ceremony in 1928. File:Ceremony of the Enthronement of His Majesty the Emperor at the Seiden7.jpg|''KЕҚrozen no hЕҚ'' worn by Emperor [[Naruhito]] during the 2019 enthronement ceremony. File:Rittaishi of Prince Naruhito.jpg|Emperor Naruhito (then Crown Prince) at his investiture. File:Emperor Takakura.jpg|Portrait of [[Emperor Takakura]] wearing the ''kЕҚrozen no hЕҚ,'' from the ''Tenshi Sekkan MyЕҚei.'' File:Tang Emperor Taizong.jpg|Portrait of [[Tang Taizong]], showing the Chinese tradition of yellow imperial robes. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kikujin no hЕҚ|ja|йәҙеЎөиўҚ}} * {{ill|ЕҢtan no hЕҚ|ja|й»„дё№иўҚ}} * {{ill|Mikado Yellow|ja|гғҹгӮ«гғүгӮӨгӮЁгғӯгғј}} == External Links == * [http://www.iz2.or.jp/fukushoku/f_disp.php?page_no=0000165 ''KЕҚrozen no hЕҚ'' replica], Costume Museum. {{Double image aside|right|Emperor Naruhito (cropped).jpg |209| Emperor Akihito 199011 1.jpg |214|Left: [[2019]] (Reiwa 1), Emperor [[Naruhito]] wearing the KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ
Right: [[1990]] (Heisei 2), Emperor [[Akihito]] wearing the same ceremonial robe during his reign.}} '''KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ''' (й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ) is a {{ill|sokutai|ja|жқҹеёҜ}} ceremonial robe worn exclusively by the [[Emperor of Japan]] during enthronement ceremonies and important rituals. It is a highly symbolic robe, dyed in the unique color known as {{ill|KЕҚrozen|ja|й»„ж«Ёжҹ“}}, reserved solely for the Emperor. The color kЕҚrozen is derived from natural dyes, specifically the bark of the {{ill|Yamahaze|ja|еұұж«Ё}} tree and the heartwood of {{ill|Sappanwood|ja|иҳҮиҠі}}. It has been described as a "reddish-yellow"{{Sfn|ж—Ҙжң¬еӣҪиӘһеӨ§иҫһе…ё|2000}} or "yellowish-brown."{{Sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}} Variations in tone are said to reflect differences in era and the wearer's age.{{Sfn|йҲҙжңЁ|1985}} In 2019, during the [[:en:2019_Japanese_imperial_transition#Enthronement_Ceremony|Sokutai Ceremony]], foreign media described the color as "brown-gold." [https://www.bbc.com/news/world-asia-50120563 'Naruhito: Japan's emperor proclaims enthronement in ancient ceremony'], BBC, October 22, 2019. == History and Origin == The origins of the KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ trace back to the [[Heian period]]. Initially formalized by Emperor [[Saga]] in {{ill|820|ja|ејҳд»Ғ11е№ҙ}}, the robe's use for specific court rituals and interactions was outlined in the imperial decree recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬зҙҖз•Ҙ}}. Before this, clothing regulations for emperors were not explicitly stated in earlier codes, such as the {{ill|YЕҚrЕҚ Code|ja|йӨҠиҖҒеҫӢд»Ө}}.{{Sfn|й»’еқӮ|1939|p=213}} KЕҚrozen's color was inspired by {{ill|Tang Dynasty|ja|е”җ}} culture but adapted uniquely in Japan. Unlike the {{ill|Zhehuangpao|zh|иөӯй»„иўҚ}} worn by Tang emperors, kЕҚrozen is associated with the radiant midday sun.{{sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}} This association is detailed in later writings, such as Takakura NagatsuneвҖҷs ''ZokushinsЕҚ HishЕҚ'' (1288), which states, "The color of the sun is befitting for the Emperor."{{sfn|жӯҰз”°|жҙҘз”°|2016|p=166}} Since the establishment of the robe's exclusive use for emperors, kЕҚrozen has been regarded as a {{ill|forbidden color|ja|зҰҒиүІ}}вҖ”strictly prohibited for use by anyone else.{{sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}} == Design and Construction == === Dyeing Process === According to the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸ}}, dyeing the kЕҚrozen fabric required a precise mixture of yamahaze bark and sappanwood. The result was a rich, subdued yellow-brown color, symbolizing the dignity of the Emperor. Due to the complexity of the process, achieving a consistent hue was challenging, even for skilled dyers.{{sfn|еҗүеІЎ|2000}} === Patterns and Textures === The robe's patterns have evolved. Initially, designs such as ''Kiritake HЕҚЕҚmon'' (paulownia, bamboo, and phoenix motifs) were used. By the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|е®Өз”әжҷӮд»Ј}}, the design transitioned to rectangular ''kakugata mon'' patterns incorporating mythical creatures like the phoenix and kirin.{{sfn|йҲҙжңЁ|1985}} These patterns remain in use today. === Fabric and Lining === Modern KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ robes are made with ''koteaya'' fabric, and their linings vary by tradition.{{sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}} == Ritual and Usage == The robe is integral to imperial rituals, including the {{ill|Enthronement of the Japanese emperor|ja|еҚідҪҚзӨјжӯЈж®ҝгҒ®е„Җ}}, {{ill|Shihohai|lt=Ritual of the Four Directions|ja|еӣӣж–№жӢқ}}, and other key {{ill|Imperial Household Shinto|ja|е®®дёӯзҘӯзҘҖ}} ceremonies. Exceptions include the {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|ж–°еҳ—зҘӯ}}, where the Emperor wears a ceremonial white robe (''gosai fuku''). == Gallery == File:Emperor Showa.jpg|[[1928]] (ShЕҚwa 3), Emperor [[ShЕҚwa]] wearing the KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ during his enthronement ceremony. File:Ceremony of the Enthronement of His Majesty the Emperor at the Seiden7.jpg|2019 (Reiwa 1), Emperor Naruhito during the ''Sokutai Ceremony.'' File:Rittaishi of Prince Naruhito.jpg|Crown Prince Naruhito's investiture ceremony. File:Emperor Takakura.jpg|Portrait of Emperor [[Takakura]], depicted in a KЕҚrozen robe. File:Tang Emperor Taizong.jpg|Tang Emperor Taizong wearing a robe similar in color to KЕҚrozen. == See Also == * {{ill|Kikujin robe|ja|йәҙеЎөиўҚ}} * {{ill|ЕҢni robe|ja|й»„дё№иўҚ}} * {{ill|Mikado yellow|ja|гғҹгӮ«гғүгӮӨгӮЁгғӯгғј|en}} == References == {{и„ҡжіЁгғҳгғ«гғ—}} {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.iz2.or.jp/fukushoku/f_disp.php?page_no=0000165 "KЕҚrozen Robes"], Costume {{Double image aside|right|Emperor Naruhito (cropped).jpg |209| Emperor Akihito 199011 1.jpg |214|Left: Emperor [[Naruhito]] wearing the ''KЕҚrozen no hЕҚ'' (й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ) in the first year of the Reiwa era (2019).
Right: Emperor [[Akihito]] (at the time) wearing the ''KЕҚrozen no hЕҚ'' in 1990 (Heisei 2).}} '''KЕҚrozen no hЕҚ''' (й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ) is the ceremonial [[court dress]] of the [[Emperor of Japan]] since the [[Heian period]]. Worn during enthronement ceremonies and significant rituals, this ''hЕҚ'' (robe) is exclusively reserved for the Emperor and is dyed in the distinctive ''kЕҚrozen'' color. ''KЕҚrozen'' is a dye made from the bark of the [[wax tree]] and the heartwood of [[sappanwood]]. Its hue is described as a reddish-yellow{{Sfn|Nihon Kokugo Daijiten|2000}} or a yellowish-brown{{Sfn|Takada|1986}}. The shade varied depending on the time period and the wearer's age{{Sfn|Suzuki|1985}}. During the [[2019 enthronement ceremony]], foreign media described the color as {{lang|ja|brown-gold}}[https://www.bbc.com/news/world-asia-50120563 "Naruhito: Japan's emperor proclaims enthronement in ancient ceremony"], BBC, October 22, 2019.. == History and Origin == The ''[[YЕҚrЕҚ Code]]'' of the [[Nara period]] regulated [[formal dress]] for the [[Crown Prince]] and officials but did not specify the attire for the Emperor{{Sfn|Kurosaka|1939|p=213}}. The ''[[ShЕҚsЕҚin]] documents'' reveal that the Emperor's court attire (''kon-e'') included ''hakuno awasehЕҚ,'' a white silk robe{{Sfn|Imperial Household Museum|1929|p=65}}{{Sfn|KondЕҚ|2019|loc=В§ 1.2 (Kindle)}}. The ''kЕҚrozen no hЕҚ'' became established as imperial attire in the 11th year of the [[KЕҚnin era]] (820 CE). [[Emperor Saga]] decreed on the first day of the second month that ''kЕҚrozen'' robes be worn for specific occasions such as [[new moon]] ceremonies, [[listening to petitions]], receptions for foreign envoys, [[:ja:еҘүе№Ј|hЕҚbei]] rituals, and seasonal banquets. For [[Shinto]] rites or visits to [[Japanese imperial tombs|imperial tombs]] in winter, ''haku-e'' (white robes) were designated; for [[New Year's]] celebrations, ''konmen-jЕ«nishЕҚ'' (еҚҒдәҢз« , twelve-embroidered robes) were used{{Sfn|Nihon Kiryaku|loc=KЕҚnin 11}}. These regulations reflected influences from [[Tang Dynasty]] [[China]], where yellow robes symbolized imperial authority. The ''kЕҚrozen'' hue was believed to represent the sun at its zenith, symbolizing imperial divinity{{Sfn|Takada|1986}}. == Design and Usage == ### Dyeing Process The ''[[Engishiki]]'' describes the materials for dyeing ''kЕҚrozen'' robes: 14 ''kin'' of wax tree bark, 11 ''kin'' of sappanwood, 2 ''shЕҚ'' of vinegar, and 3 ''koku'' of ash, with 8 bundles of firewood as fuel. The result was a subdued yellow-brown or reddish-brown hue, rather than a bright sunlit tone. Variations occurred due to differences in craftsmanship and wearer specifications{{Sfn|Suzuki|1985}}. ### Patterns Early ''kЕҚrozen no hЕҚ'' designs featured all-over motifs (''sЕҚmon'') of ''paulownia,'' ''bamboo,'' and ''phoenix'' (''kiritake hЕҚЕҚ''){{Sfn|Suzuki|1983|p=254}}. The [[Heian period]] saw the introduction of rectangular designs (''hakogata mon''), which are preserved in the robes of later emperors and remain in modern usage. == Gallery == File:Emperor Showa.jpg|Emperor [[ShЕҚwa]] wearing the ''kЕҚrozen no hЕҚ'' during his enthronement ceremony in 1928. File:Ceremony of the Enthronement of His Majesty the Emperor at the Seiden7.jpg|''KЕҚrozen no hЕҚ'' worn by Emperor [[Naruhito]] during the 2019 enthronement ceremony. File:Rittaishi of Prince Naruhito.jpg|Emperor Naruhito (then Crown Prince) at his investiture. File:Emperor Takakura.jpg|Portrait of [[Emperor Takakura]] wearing the ''kЕҚrozen no hЕҚ,'' from the ''Tenshi Sekkan MyЕҚei.'' File:Tang Emperor Taizong.jpg|Portrait of [[Tang Taizong]], showing the Chinese tradition of yellow imperial robes. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kikujin no hЕҚ|ja|йәҙеЎөиўҚ}} * {{ill|ЕҢtan no hЕҚ|ja|й»„дё№иўҚ}} * {{ill|Mikado Yellow|ja|гғҹгӮ«гғүгӮӨгӮЁгғӯгғј}} == External Links == * [http://www.iz2.or.jp/fukushoku/f_disp.php?page_no=0000165 ''KЕҚrozen no hЕҚ'' replica], Costume Museum. File:Emperor Takakura.jpg|Portrait of [[Emperor Takakura]] wearing the ''kЕҚrozen no hЕҚ,'' from the ''Tenshi Sekkan MyЕҚei.'' {{Double image aside|right|Emperor Naruhito (cropped).jpg |209| Emperor Akihito 199011 1.jpg |214|Left: [[2019]] (Reiwa 1), Emperor [[Naruhito]] wearing the KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ
Right: [[1990]] (Heisei 2), Emperor [[Akihito]] wearing the same ceremonial robe during his reign.}} '''KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ''' (й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ) is a {{ill|sokutai|ja|жқҹеёҜ}} ceremonial robe worn exclusively by the [[Emperor of Japan]] during enthronement ceremonies and important rituals. It is a highly symbolic robe, dyed in the unique color known as {{ill|KЕҚrozen|ja|й»„ж«Ёжҹ“}}, reserved solely for the Emperor. The color kЕҚrozen is derived from natural dyes, specifically the bark of the {{ill|Yamahaze|ja|еұұж«Ё}} tree and the heartwood of {{ill|Sappanwood|ja|иҳҮиҠі}}. It has been described as a "reddish-yellow"{{Sfn|ж—Ҙжң¬еӣҪиӘһеӨ§иҫһе…ё|2000}} or "yellowish-brown."{{Sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}} Variations in tone are said to reflect differences in era and the wearer's age.{{Sfn|йҲҙжңЁ|1985}} In 2019, during the [[:en:2019_Japanese_imperial_transition#Enthronement_Ceremony|Sokutai Ceremony]], foreign media described the color as "brown-gold." [https://www.bbc.com/news/world-asia-50120563 'Naruhito: Japan's emperor proclaims enthronement in ancient ceremony'], BBC, October 22, 2019. == History and Origin == The origins of the KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ trace back to the [[Heian period]]. Initially formalized by Emperor [[Saga]] in {{ill|820|ja|ејҳд»Ғ11е№ҙ}}, the robe's use for specific court rituals and interactions was outlined in the imperial decree recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬зҙҖз•Ҙ}}. Before this, clothing regulations for emperors were not explicitly stated in earlier codes, such as the {{ill|YЕҚrЕҚ Code|ja|йӨҠиҖҒеҫӢд»Ө}}.{{Sfn|й»’еқӮ|1939|p=213}} KЕҚrozen's color was inspired by {{ill|Tang Dynasty|ja|е”җ}} culture but adapted uniquely in Japan. Unlike the {{ill|Zhehuangpao|zh|иөӯй»„иўҚ}} worn by Tang emperors, kЕҚrozen is associated with the radiant midday sun.{{sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}} This association is detailed in later writings, such as Takakura NagatsuneвҖҷs ''ZokushinsЕҚ HishЕҚ'' (1288), which states, "The color of the sun is befitting for the Emperor."{{sfn|жӯҰз”°|жҙҘз”°|2016|p=166}} Since the establishment of the robe's exclusive use for emperors, kЕҚrozen has been regarded as a {{ill|forbidden color|ja|зҰҒиүІ}}вҖ”strictly prohibited for use by anyone else.{{sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}} == Design and Construction == === Dyeing Process === According to the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸ}}, dyeing the kЕҚrozen fabric required a precise mixture of yamahaze bark and sappanwood. The result was a rich, subdued yellow-brown color, symbolizing the dignity of the Emperor. Due to the complexity of the process, achieving a consistent hue was challenging, even for skilled dyers.{{sfn|еҗүеІЎ|2000}} === Patterns and Textures === The robe's patterns have evolved. Initially, designs such as ''Kiritake HЕҚЕҚmon'' (paulownia, bamboo, and phoenix motifs) were used. By the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|е®Өз”әжҷӮд»Ј}}, the design transitioned to rectangular ''kakugata mon'' patterns incorporating mythical creatures like the phoenix and kirin.{{sfn|йҲҙжңЁ|1985}} These patterns remain in use today. === Fabric and Lining === Modern KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ robes are made with ''koteaya'' fabric, and their linings vary by tradition.{{sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}} == Ritual and Usage == The robe is integral to imperial rituals, including the {{ill|Enthronement of the Japanese emperor|ja|еҚідҪҚзӨјжӯЈж®ҝгҒ®е„Җ}}, {{ill|Shihohai|lt=Ritual of the Four Directions|ja|еӣӣж–№жӢқ}}, and other key {{ill|Imperial Household Shinto|ja|е®®дёӯзҘӯзҘҖ}} ceremonies. Exceptions include the {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|ж–°еҳ—зҘӯ}}, where the Emperor wears a ceremonial white robe (''gosai fuku''). == Gallery == File:Emperor Showa.jpg|[[1928]] (ShЕҚwa 3), Emperor [[ShЕҚwa]] wearing the KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ during his enthronement ceremony. File:Ceremony of the Enthronement of His Majesty the Emperor at the Seiden7.jpg|2019 (Reiwa 1), Emperor Naruhito during the ''Sokutai Ceremony.'' File:Rittaishi of Prince Naruhito.jpg|Crown Prince Naruhito's investiture ceremony. File:Emperor Takakura.jpg|Portrait of Emperor [[Takakura]], depicted in a KЕҚrozen robe. File:Tang Emperor Taizong.jpg|Tang Emperor Taizong wearing a robe similar in color to KЕҚrozen. == See Also == * {{ill|Kikujin robe|ja|йәҙеЎөиўҚ}} * {{ill|ЕҢni robe|ja|й»„дё№иўҚ}} * {{ill|Mikado yellow|ja|гғҹгӮ«гғүгӮӨгӮЁгғӯгғј|en}} == References == {{и„ҡжіЁгғҳгғ«гғ—}} {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.iz2.or.jp/fukushoku/f_disp.php?page_no=0000165 "KЕҚrozen Robes"], Costume Museum (replica of modern KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ). {{DEFAULTSORT:KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Kourozen-no-gohou]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] {{Double image aside|right|Emperor Naruhito (cropped).jpg |209| Emperor Akihito 199011 1.jpg |214|Left: [[2019]] (Reiwa 1), Emperor [[Naruhito]] wearing the KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ
Right: [[1990]] (Heisei 2), Emperor [[Akihito]] wearing the same ceremonial robe during his reign.}} '''KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ''' (й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ) is a {{ill|sokutai|ja|жқҹеёҜ}} ceremonial robe worn exclusively by the [[Emperor of Japan]] during enthronement ceremonies and important rituals. It is a highly symbolic robe, dyed in the unique color known as {{ill|KЕҚrozen|ja|й»„ж«Ёжҹ“}}, reserved solely for the Emperor. The color kЕҚrozen is derived from natural dyes, specifically the bark of the {{ill|Yamahaze|ja|еұұж«Ё}} tree and the heartwood of {{ill|Sappanwood|ja|иҳҮиҠі}}. It has been described as a "reddish-yellow"{{Sfn|ж—Ҙжң¬еӣҪиӘһеӨ§иҫһе…ё|2000}} or "yellowish-brown."{{Sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}} Variations in tone are said to reflect differences in era and the wearer's age.{{Sfn|йҲҙжңЁ|1985}} In 2019, during the [[:en:2019_Japanese_imperial_transition#Enthronement_Ceremony|Sokutai Ceremony]], foreign media described the color as "brown-gold." [https://www.bbc.com/news/world-asia-50120563 'Naruhito: Japan's emperor proclaims enthronement in ancient ceremony'], BBC, October 22, 2019. == History and Origin == The origins of the KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ trace back to the [[Heian period]]. Initially formalized by Emperor [[Saga]] in {{ill|820|ja|ејҳд»Ғ11е№ҙ}}, the robe's use for specific court rituals and interactions was outlined in the imperial decree recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬зҙҖз•Ҙ}}. Before this, clothing regulations for emperors were not explicitly stated in earlier codes, such as the {{ill|YЕҚrЕҚ Code|ja|йӨҠиҖҒеҫӢд»Ө}}.{{Sfn|й»’еқӮ|1939|p=213}} KЕҚrozen's color was inspired by {{ill|Tang Dynasty|ja|е”җ}} culture but adapted uniquely in Japan. Unlike the {{ill|Zhehuangpao|zh|иөӯй»„иўҚ}} worn by Tang emperors, kЕҚrozen is associated with the radiant midday sun.{{sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}} This association is detailed in later writings, such as Takakura NagatsuneвҖҷs ''ZokushinsЕҚ HishЕҚ'' (1288), which states, "The color of the sun is befitting for the Emperor."{{sfn|жӯҰз”°|жҙҘз”°|2016|p=166}} Since the establishment of the robe's exclusive use for emperors, kЕҚrozen has been regarded as a {{ill|forbidden color|ja|зҰҒиүІ}}вҖ”strictly prohibited for use by anyone else.{{sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}} == Design and Construction == === Dyeing Process === According to the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸ}}, dyeing the kЕҚrozen fabric required a precise mixture of yamahaze bark and sappanwood. The result was a rich, subdued yellow-brown color, symbolizing the dignity of the Emperor. Due to the complexity of the process, achieving a consistent hue was challenging, even for skilled dyers.{{sfn|еҗүеІЎ|2000}} === Patterns and Textures === The robe's patterns have evolved. Initially, designs such as ''Kiritake HЕҚЕҚmon'' (paulownia, bamboo, and phoenix motifs) were used. By the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|е®Өз”әжҷӮд»Ј}}, the design transitioned to rectangular ''kakugata mon'' patterns incorporating mythical creatures like the phoenix and kirin.{{sfn|йҲҙжңЁ|1985}} These patterns remain in use today. === Fabric and Lining === Modern KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ robes are made with ''koteaya'' fabric, and their linings vary by tradition.{{sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}} == Ritual and Usage == The robe is integral to imperial rituals, including the {{ill|Enthronement of the Japanese emperor|ja|еҚідҪҚзӨјжӯЈж®ҝгҒ®е„Җ}}, {{ill|Shihohai|lt=Ritual of the Four Directions|ja|еӣӣж–№жӢқ}}, and other key {{ill|Imperial Household Shinto|ja|е®®дёӯзҘӯзҘҖ}} ceremonies. Exceptions include the {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|ж–°еҳ—зҘӯ}}, where the Emperor wears a ceremonial white robe (''gosai fuku''). == Gallery == File:Emperor Showa.jpg|[[1928]] (ShЕҚwa 3), Emperor [[ShЕҚwa]] wearing the KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ during his enthronement ceremony. File:Ceremony of the Enthronement of His Majesty the Emperor at the Seiden7.jpg|2019 (Reiwa 1), Emperor Naruhito during the ''Sokutai Ceremony.'' File:Rittaishi of Prince Naruhito.jpg|Crown Prince Naruhito's investiture ceremony. File:Emperor Takakura.jpg|Portrait of [[Emperor Takakura]], depicted in a KЕҚrozen robe. File:Tang Emperor Taizong.jpg|Tang Emperor Taizong wearing a robe similar in color to KЕҚrozen. == See Also == * {{ill|Kikujin robe|ja|йәҙеЎөиўҚ}} * {{ill|ЕҢni robe|ja|й»„дё№иўҚ}} * {{ill|Mikado yellow|ja|гғҹгӮ«гғүгӮӨгӮЁгғӯгғј|en}} == References == {{и„ҡжіЁгғҳгғ«гғ—}} {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.iz2.or.jp/fukushoku/f_disp.php?page_no=0000165 "KЕҚrozen Robes"], Costume Museum (replica of modern KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ). {{DEFAULTSORT:KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ}} [[Category:Kourozen-no-gohou]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kourozen-no-gohou]] [[Category:Kourozen-no-gohou]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kourozen-no-gohou]] [[Category:Kourozen-no-gohou]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kourozen-no-gohou]] [[Category:Kourozen-no-gohou]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kourozen-no-gohou]] [[Category:Kourozen-no-gohou]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kourozen-no-gohou]] [[Category:Kourozen-no-gohou]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kourozen-no-gohou]] [[Category:Kourozen-no-gohou]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Kourozen-no-gohou]][[Category:Court and aristocratic attire]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Kourozen-no-gohou]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Kourozen-no-gohou]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Kourozen-no-gohou]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ]]

[[File:Emperor Naruhito (cropped).jpg|thumb]]'''KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ''' (й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ) is a {{ill|sokutai|ja|жқҹеёҜ}} ceremonial robe worn exclusively by the [[Emperor of Japan]] during enthronement ceremonies and important rituals. It is a highly symbolic robe, dyed in the unique color known as {{ill|KЕҚrozen|ja|й»„ж«Ёжҹ“}}, reserved solely for the Emperor.
The color kЕҚrozen is derived from natural dyes, specifically the bark of the {{ill|Yamahaze|ja|еұұж«Ё}} tree and the heartwood of {{ill|Sappanwood|ja|иҳҮиҠі}}. It has been described as a "reddish-yellow"{{Sfn|ж—Ҙжң¬еӣҪиӘһеӨ§иҫһе…ё|2000}} or "yellowish-brown."{{Sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}} Variations in tone are said to reflect differences in era and the wearer's age.{{Sfn|йҲҙжңЁ|1985}} In 2019, during the [[:en:2019_Japanese_imperial_transition#Enthronement_Ceremony|Sokutai Ceremony]], foreign media described the color as "brown-gold." <ref>[https://www.bbc.com/news/world-asia-50120563 'Naruhito: Japan's emperor proclaims enthronement in ancient ceremony'], BBC, October 22, 2019.</ref>
== History and Origin ==
The origins of the KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ trace back to the [[Heian period]]. Initially formalized by Emperor [[Saga]] in {{ill|820|ja|ејҳд»Ғ11е№ҙ}}, the robe's use for specific court rituals and interactions was outlined in the imperial decree recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬зҙҖз•Ҙ}}. Before this, clothing regulations for emperors were not explicitly stated in earlier codes, such as the {{ill|YЕҚrЕҚ Code|ja|йӨҠиҖҒеҫӢд»Ө}}.{{Sfn|й»’еқӮ|1939|p=213}}
KЕҚrozen's color was inspired by {{ill|Tang Dynasty|ja|е”җ}} culture but adapted uniquely in Japan. Unlike the {{ill|Zhehuangpao|zh|иөӯй»„иўҚ}} worn by Tang emperors, kЕҚrozen is associated with the radiant midday sun.{{sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}} This association is detailed in later writings, such as Takakura NagatsuneвҖҷs ''ZokushinsЕҚ HishЕҚ'' (1288), which states, "The color of the sun is befitting for the Emperor."{{sfn|жӯҰз”°|жҙҘз”°|2016|p=166}}
Since the establishment of the robe's exclusive use for emperors, kЕҚrozen has been regarded as a {{ill|forbidden color|ja|зҰҒиүІ}}вҖ”strictly prohibited for use by anyone else.{{sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}}
== Design and Construction ==
=== Dyeing Process === According to the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸ}}, dyeing the kЕҚrozen fabric required a precise mixture of yamahaze bark and sappanwood. The result was a rich, subdued yellow-brown color, symbolizing the dignity of the Emperor. Due to the complexity of the process, achieving a consistent hue was challenging, even for skilled dyers.{{sfn|еҗүеІЎ|2000}}
=== Patterns and Textures === The robe's patterns have evolved. Initially, designs such as ''Kiritake HЕҚЕҚmon'' (paulownia, bamboo, and phoenix motifs) were used. By the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|е®Өз”әжҷӮд»Ј}}, the design transitioned to rectangular ''kakugata mon'' patterns incorporating mythical creatures like the phoenix and kirin.{{sfn|йҲҙжңЁ|1985}} These patterns remain in use today.
=== Fabric and Lining === Modern KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ robes are made with ''koteaya'' fabric, and their linings vary by tradition.{{sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}}
== Ritual and Usage == The robe is integral to imperial rituals, including the {{ill|Enthronement of the Japanese emperor|ja|еҚідҪҚзӨјжӯЈж®ҝгҒ®е„Җ}}, {{ill|Shihohai|lt=Ritual of the Four Directions|ja|еӣӣж–№жӢқ}}, and other key {{ill|Imperial Household Shinto|ja|е®®дёӯзҘӯзҘҖ}} ceremonies. Exceptions include the {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|ж–°еҳ—зҘӯ}}, where the Emperor wears a ceremonial white robe (''gosai fuku'').
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Emperor Showa.jpg|[[1928]] (ShЕҚwa 3), Emperor [[ShЕҚwa]] wearing the KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ during his enthronement ceremony. File:Ceremony of the Enthronement of His Majesty the Emperor at the Seiden7.jpg|2019 (Reiwa 1), Emperor Naruhito during the ''Sokutai Ceremony.'' File:Rittaishi of Prince Naruhito.jpg|Crown Prince Naruhito's investiture ceremony. File:Emperor Takakura.jpg|Portrait of [[Emperor Takakura]], depicted in a KЕҚrozen robe. File:Tang Emperor Taizong.jpg|Tang Emperor Taizong wearing a robe similar in color to KЕҚrozen. </gallery>

[[File:Emperor Naruhito (cropped).jpg|thumb]]'''KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ''' (й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ) is a {{ill|sokutai|ja|жқҹеёҜ}} ceremonial robe worn exclusively by the [[Emperor of Japan]] during enthronement ceremonies and important rituals. It is a highly symbolic robe, dyed in the unique color known as {{ill|KЕҚrozen|ja|й»„ж«Ёжҹ“}}, reserved solely for the Emperor.
The color kЕҚrozen is derived from natural dyes, specifically the bark of the {{ill|Yamahaze|ja|еұұж«Ё}} tree and the heartwood of {{ill|Sappanwood|ja|иҳҮиҠі}}. It has been described as a "reddish-yellow"{{Sfn|ж—Ҙжң¬еӣҪиӘһеӨ§иҫһе…ё|2000}} or "yellowish-brown."{{Sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}} Variations in tone are said to reflect differences in era and the wearer's age.{{Sfn|йҲҙжңЁ|1985}} In 2019, during the [[:en:2019_Japanese_imperial_transition#Enthronement_Ceremony|Sokutai Ceremony]], foreign media described the color as "brown-gold." <ref>[https://www.bbc.com/news/world-asia-50120563 'Naruhito: Japan's emperor proclaims enthronement in ancient ceremony'], BBC, October 22, 2019.</ref>
== History and Origin ==
The origins of the KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ trace back to the [[Heian period]]. Initially formalized by Emperor [[Saga]] in {{ill|820|ja|ејҳд»Ғ11е№ҙ}}, the robe's use for specific court rituals and interactions was outlined in the imperial decree recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬зҙҖз•Ҙ}}. Before this, clothing regulations for emperors were not explicitly stated in earlier codes, such as the {{ill|YЕҚrЕҚ Code|ja|йӨҠиҖҒеҫӢд»Ө}}.{{Sfn|й»’еқӮ|1939|p=213}}
KЕҚrozen's color was inspired by {{ill|Tang Dynasty|ja|е”җ}} culture but adapted uniquely in Japan. Unlike the {{ill|Zhehuangpao|zh|иөӯй»„иўҚ}} worn by Tang emperors, kЕҚrozen is associated with the radiant midday sun.{{sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}} This association is detailed in later writings, such as Takakura NagatsuneвҖҷs ''ZokushinsЕҚ HishЕҚ'' (1288), which states, "The color of the sun is befitting for the Emperor."{{sfn|жӯҰз”°|жҙҘз”°|2016|p=166}}
Since the establishment of the robe's exclusive use for emperors, kЕҚrozen has been regarded as a {{ill|forbidden color|ja|зҰҒиүІ}}вҖ”strictly prohibited for use by anyone else.{{sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}}
== Design and Construction ==
=== Dyeing Process === According to the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸ}}, dyeing the kЕҚrozen fabric required a precise mixture of yamahaze bark and sappanwood. The result was a rich, subdued yellow-brown color, symbolizing the dignity of the Emperor. Due to the complexity of the process, achieving a consistent hue was challenging, even for skilled dyers.{{sfn|еҗүеІЎ|2000}}
=== Patterns and Textures === The robe's patterns have evolved. Initially, designs such as ''Kiritake HЕҚЕҚmon'' (paulownia, bamboo, and phoenix motifs) were used. By the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|е®Өз”әжҷӮд»Ј}}, the design transitioned to rectangular ''kakugata mon'' patterns incorporating mythical creatures like the phoenix and kirin.{{sfn|йҲҙжңЁ|1985}} These patterns remain in use today.
=== Fabric and Lining === Modern KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ robes are made with ''koteaya'' fabric, and their linings vary by tradition.{{sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}}
== Ritual and Usage == The robe is integral to imperial rituals, including the {{ill|Enthronement of the Japanese emperor|ja|еҚідҪҚзӨјжӯЈж®ҝгҒ®е„Җ}}, {{ill|Shihohai|lt=Ritual of the Four Directions|ja|еӣӣж–№жӢқ}}, and other key {{ill|Imperial Household Shinto|ja|е®®дёӯзҘӯзҘҖ}} ceremonies. Exceptions include the {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|ж–°еҳ—зҘӯ}}, where the Emperor wears a ceremonial white robe (''gosai fuku'').
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Emperor Showa.jpg|[[1928]] (ShЕҚwa 3), Emperor [[ShЕҚwa]] wearing the KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ during his enthronement ceremony. File:Ceremony of the Enthronement of His Majesty the Emperor at the Seiden7.jpg|2019 (Reiwa 1), Emperor Naruhito during the ''Sokutai Ceremony.'' File:Rittaishi of Prince Naruhito.jpg|Crown Prince Naruhito's investiture ceremony. File:Emperor Takakura.jpg|Portrait of [[Emperor Takakura]], depicted in a KЕҚrozen robe. File:Tang Emperor Taizong.jpg|Tang Emperor Taizong wearing a robe similar in color to KЕҚrozen. </gallery>
<ref></ref>== See Also == * {{ill|Kikujin robe|ja|йәҙеЎөиўҚ}} * {{ill|ЕҢni robe|ja|й»„дё№иўҚ}} * [[:en:Mikado yellow|Mikado yellow]]

Due to the complexity of the process, achieving a consistent hue was challenging, even for skilled dyers.{{sfn|еҗүеІЎ|2000}}

By the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|е®Өз”әжҷӮд»Ј}}, the design transitioned to rectangular ''kakugata mon'' patterns incorporating mythical creatures like the phoenix and kirin.{{sfn|йҲҙжңЁ|1985}} These patterns remain in use today.
=== Fabric and Lining === Modern KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ robes are made with ''koteaya'' fabric, and their linings vary by tradition.{{sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}}
== Ritual and Usage == The robe is integral to imperial rituals, including the {{ill|Enthronement of the Japanese emperor|ja|еҚідҪҚзӨјжӯЈж®ҝгҒ®е„Җ}}, {{ill|Shihohai|lt=Ritual of the Four Directions|ja|еӣӣж–№жӢқ}}, and other key {{ill|Imperial Household Shinto|ja|е®®дёӯзҘӯзҘҖ}} ceremonies. Exceptions include the {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|ж–°еҳ—зҘӯ}}, where the Emperor wears a ceremonial white robe (''gosai fuku'').
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Emperor Showa.jpg|[[1928]] (ShЕҚwa 3), Emperor [[ShЕҚwa]] wearing the KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ during his enthronement ceremony. File:Ceremony of the Enthronement of His Majesty the Emperor at the Seiden7.jpg|2019 (Reiwa 1), Emperor Naruhito during the ''Sokutai Ceremony.'' File:Rittaishi of Prince Naruhito.jpg|Crown Prince Naruhito's investiture ceremony. File:Emperor Takakura.jpg|Portrait of [[Emperor Takakura]], depicted in a KЕҚrozen robe. File:Tang Emperor Taizong.jpg|Tang Emperor Taizong wearing a robe similar in color to KЕҚrozen. </gallery>
