revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:KЕҚrozen no gohЕҚ]][[ja:й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ]][[zh:й»„ж Ңжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11677782]] Wikidata name: KЕҚrozen no gohЕҚ [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Emperor Naruhito (cropped).jpg|thumb]]'''KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ''' (й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ) is a [[:en:Sokutai|sokutai]] ceremonial robe worn exclusively by the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]] during enthronement ceremonies and important rituals. It is a highly symbolic robe, dyed in the unique color known as {{ill|KЕҚrozen|ja|й»„ж«Ёжҹ“|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KЕҚrozen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ}}, reserved solely for the Emperor.
The color kЕҚrozen is derived from natural dyes, specifically the bark of the {{ill|Yamahaze|ja|еұұж«Ё|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamahaze|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} tree and the heartwood of {{ill|Sappanwood|ja|иҳҮиҠі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sappanwood|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮ№гӮӘгӮҰ|1=Biancaea sappan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56088062}}. It has been described as a "reddish-yellow"{{Sfn|ж—Ҙжң¬еӣҪиӘһеӨ§иҫһе…ё|2000}} or "yellowish-brown."{{Sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}} Variations in tone are said to reflect differences in era and the wearer's age.{{Sfn|йҲҙжңЁ|1985}} In 2019, during the [[:en:2019_Japanese_imperial_transition#Enthronement_Ceremony|Sokutai Ceremony]], foreign media described the color as "brown-gold." <ref>[https://www.bbc.com/news/world-asia-50120563 'Naruhito: Japan's emperor proclaims enthronement in ancient ceremony'], BBC, October 22, 2019.</ref>
== History and Origin ==
The origins of the KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ trace back to the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. Initially formalized by Emperor [[:en:Saga|Saga]] in [[:en:820|820]], the robe's use for specific court rituals and interactions was outlined in the imperial decree recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Kiryaku|Nihon Kiryaku]]. Before this, clothing regulations for emperors were not explicitly stated in earlier codes, such as the [[:en:YЕҚrЕҚ Code|YЕҚrЕҚ Code]].{{Sfn|й»’еқӮ|1939|p=213}}
KЕҚrozen's color was inspired by [[:en:Tang dynasty|Tang Dynasty]] culture but adapted uniquely in Japan. Unlike the {{ill|Zhehuangpao|zh|иөӯй»„иўҚ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhehuangpao|qq=}} worn by Tang emperors, kЕҚrozen is associated with the radiant midday sun.{{sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}} This association is detailed in later writings, such as Takakura NagatsuneвҖҷs ''ZokushinsЕҚ HishЕҚ'' (1288), which states, "The color of the sun is befitting for the Emperor."{{sfn|жӯҰз”°|жҙҘз”°|2016|p=166}}
Since the establishment of the robe's exclusive use for emperors, kЕҚrozen has been regarded as a [[:en:Forbidden colors (Japan)|Forbidden color]]вҖ”strictly prohibited for use by anyone else.{{sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}}
== Design and Construction ==
=== Dyeing Process === According to the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], dyeing the kЕҚrozen fabric required a precise mixture of yamahaze bark and sappanwood. The result was a rich, subdued yellow-brown color, symbolizing the dignity of the Emperor. Due to the complexity of the process, achieving a consistent hue was challenging, even for skilled dyers.{{sfn|еҗүеІЎ|2000}}
=== Patterns and Textures === The robe's patterns have evolved. Initially, designs such as ''Kiritake HЕҚЕҚmon'' (paulownia, bamboo, and phoenix motifs) were used. By the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the design transitioned to rectangular ''kakugata mon'' patterns incorporating mythical creatures like the phoenix and kirin.{{sfn|йҲҙжңЁ|1985}} These patterns remain in use today.
=== Fabric and Lining === Modern KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ robes are made with ''koteaya'' fabric, and their linings vary by tradition.{{sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}}
== Ritual and Usage == The robe is integral to imperial rituals, including the {{ill|Enthronement of the Japanese emperor|ja|еҚідҪҚзӨјжӯЈж®ҝгҒ®е„Җ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enthronement of the Japanese emperor|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, {{ill|Shihohai|lt=Ritual of the Four Directions|ja|еӣӣж–№жӢқ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shihohai|qq=}}, and other key [[:en:Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|Ceremonies of the Japanese Imperial Palace]] ceremonies. Exceptions include the [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]], where the Emperor wears a ceremonial white robe (''gosai fuku'').
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Emperor Showa.jpg|[[:en:1928|1928]] (ShЕҚwa 3), Emperor [[:en:ShЕҚwa|ShЕҚwa]] wearing the KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ during his enthronement ceremony. File:Ceremony of the Enthronement of His Majesty the Emperor at the Seiden7.jpg|2019 (Reiwa 1), Emperor Naruhito during the ''Sokutai Ceremony.'' File:Rittaishi of Prince Naruhito.jpg|Crown Prince Naruhito's investiture ceremony. File:Emperor Takakura.jpg|Portrait of [[:en:Emperor Takakura|Emperor Takakura]], depicted in a KЕҚrozen robe. File:Tang Emperor Taizong.jpg|Tang Emperor Taizong wearing a robe similar in color to KЕҚrozen. </gallery>
<ref></ref> == See Also == * {{ill|Kikujin robe|ja|йәҙеЎөиўҚ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikujin robe|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to йә№еЎөиўҚ}} * {{ill|ЕҢni robe|ja|й»„дё№иўҚ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢni robe|qq=}} * [[:en:Mikado yellow|Mikado yellow]]
== References ==
{{Reflist|2}}
== External Links == * [http://www.iz2.or.jp/fukushoku/f_disp.php?page_no=0000165 "KЕҚrozen Robes"], Costume Museum (replica of modern KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ).

<!--KЕҚrozen--><!--KЕҚrozen--><!--KЕҚrozen--><!--KЕҚrozen--><!--KЕҚrozen--><!--KЕҚrozen--><!--KЕҚrozen--> [[Category:Traditional clothing of the Heian period]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|KЕҚrozen no gohЕҚ}} [[zh:й»„ж Ңжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ]] {{wikidata link|Q11677782}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:KЕҚrozen no gohЕҚ]][[ja:й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ]][[zh:й»„ж Ңжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11677782]] Wikidata name: KЕҚrozen no gohЕҚ [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Emperor Naruhito (cropped).jpg|thumb]]'''KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ''' (й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ) is a [[:en:Sokutai|sokutai]] ceremonial robe worn exclusively by the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]] during enthronement ceremonies and important rituals. It is a highly symbolic robe, dyed in the unique color known as {{ill|KЕҚrozen|ja|й»„ж«Ёжҹ“|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KЕҚrozen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ}}, reserved solely for the Emperor.
The color kЕҚrozen is derived from natural dyes, specifically the bark of the {{ill|Yamahaze|ja|еұұж«Ё|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamahaze|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} tree and the heartwood of {{ill|Sappanwood|ja|иҳҮиҠі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sappanwood|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮ№гӮӘгӮҰ|1=Biancaea sappan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56088062}}. It has been described as a "reddish-yellow"{{Sfn|ж—Ҙжң¬еӣҪиӘһеӨ§иҫһе…ё|2000}} or "yellowish-brown."{{Sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}} Variations in tone are said to reflect differences in era and the wearer's age.{{Sfn|йҲҙжңЁ|1985}} In 2019, during the [[:en:2019_Japanese_imperial_transition#Enthronement_Ceremony|Sokutai Ceremony]], foreign media described the color as "brown-gold." <ref>[https://www.bbc.com/news/world-asia-50120563 'Naruhito: Japan's emperor proclaims enthronement in ancient ceremony'], BBC, October 22, 2019.</ref>
== History and Origin ==
The origins of the KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ trace back to the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. Initially formalized by Emperor [[:en:Saga|Saga]] in [[:en:820|820]], the robe's use for specific court rituals and interactions was outlined in the imperial decree recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Kiryaku|Nihon Kiryaku]]. Before this, clothing regulations for emperors were not explicitly stated in earlier codes, such as the [[:en:YЕҚrЕҚ Code|YЕҚrЕҚ Code]].{{Sfn|й»’еқӮ|1939|p=213}}
KЕҚrozen's color was inspired by [[:en:Tang dynasty|Tang Dynasty]] culture but adapted uniquely in Japan. Unlike the {{ill|Zhehuangpao|zh|иөӯй»„иўҚ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhehuangpao|qq=}} worn by Tang emperors, kЕҚrozen is associated with the radiant midday sun.{{sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}} This association is detailed in later writings, such as Takakura NagatsuneвҖҷs ''ZokushinsЕҚ HishЕҚ'' (1288), which states, "The color of the sun is befitting for the Emperor."{{sfn|жӯҰз”°|жҙҘз”°|2016|p=166}}
Since the establishment of the robe's exclusive use for emperors, kЕҚrozen has been regarded as a [[:en:Forbidden colors (Japan)|Forbidden color]]вҖ”strictly prohibited for use by anyone else.{{sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}}
== Design and Construction ==
=== Dyeing Process === According to the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], dyeing the kЕҚrozen fabric required a precise mixture of yamahaze bark and sappanwood. The result was a rich, subdued yellow-brown color, symbolizing the dignity of the Emperor. Due to the complexity of the process, achieving a consistent hue was challenging, even for skilled dyers.{{sfn|еҗүеІЎ|2000}}
=== Patterns and Textures === The robe's patterns have evolved. Initially, designs such as ''Kiritake HЕҚЕҚmon'' (paulownia, bamboo, and phoenix motifs) were used. By the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the design transitioned to rectangular ''kakugata mon'' patterns incorporating mythical creatures like the phoenix and kirin.{{sfn|йҲҙжңЁ|1985}} These patterns remain in use today.
=== Fabric and Lining === Modern KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ robes are made with ''koteaya'' fabric, and their linings vary by tradition.{{sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}}
== Ritual and Usage == The robe is integral to imperial rituals, including the {{ill|Enthronement of the Japanese emperor|ja|еҚідҪҚзӨјжӯЈж®ҝгҒ®е„Җ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enthronement of the Japanese emperor|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, {{ill|Shihohai|lt=Ritual of the Four Directions|ja|еӣӣж–№жӢқ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shihohai|qq=}}, and other key [[:en:Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|Ceremonies of the Japanese Imperial Palace]] ceremonies. Exceptions include the [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]], where the Emperor wears a ceremonial white robe (''gosai fuku'').
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Emperor Showa.jpg|[[:en:1928|1928]] (ShЕҚwa 3), Emperor [[:en:ShЕҚwa|ShЕҚwa]] wearing the KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ during his enthronement ceremony. File:Ceremony of the Enthronement of His Majesty the Emperor at the Seiden7.jpg|2019 (Reiwa 1), Emperor Naruhito during the ''Sokutai Ceremony.'' File:Rittaishi of Prince Naruhito.jpg|Crown Prince Naruhito's investiture ceremony. File:Emperor Takakura.jpg|Portrait of [[:en:Emperor Takakura|Emperor Takakura]], depicted in a KЕҚrozen robe. File:Tang Emperor Taizong.jpg|Tang Emperor Taizong wearing a robe similar in color to KЕҚrozen. </gallery>
<ref></ref> == See Also == * {{ill|Kikujin robe|ja|йәҙеЎөиўҚ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikujin robe|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to йә№еЎөиўҚ}} * {{ill|ЕҢni robe|ja|й»„дё№иўҚ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢni robe|qq=}} * [[:en:Mikado yellow|Mikado yellow]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:KЕҚrozen no gohЕҚ]][[ja:й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ]][[zh:й»„ж Ңжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:KЕҚrozen no gohЕҚ]][[ja:й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ]][[zh:й»„ж Ңжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:KЕҚrozen no gohЕҚ]][[ja:й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ]][[zh:й»„ж Ңжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11677782]] Wikidata name: KЕҚrozen no gohЕҚ [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Emperor Naruhito (cropped).jpg|thumb]]'''KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ''' (й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ) is a [[:en:Sokutai|sokutai]] ceremonial robe worn exclusively by the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]] during enthronement ceremonies and important rituals. It is a highly symbolic robe, dyed in the unique color known as {{ill|KЕҚrozen|ja|й»„ж«Ёжҹ“|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KЕҚrozen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ}}, reserved solely for the Emperor.
The color kЕҚrozen is derived from natural dyes, specifically the bark of the {{ill|Yamahaze|ja|еұұж«Ё|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamahaze|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} tree and the heartwood of {{ill|Sappanwood|ja|иҳҮиҠі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sappanwood|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮ№гӮӘгӮҰ|1=Biancaea sappan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56088062}}. It has been described as a "reddish-yellow"{{Sfn|ж—Ҙжң¬еӣҪиӘһеӨ§иҫһе…ё|2000}} or "yellowish-brown."{{Sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}} Variations in tone are said to reflect differences in era and the wearer's age.{{Sfn|йҲҙжңЁ|1985}} In 2019, during the [[:en:2019_Japanese_imperial_transition#Enthronement_Ceremony|Sokutai Ceremony]], foreign media described the color as "brown-gold." <ref>[https://www.bbc.com/news/world-asia-50120563 'Naruhito: Japan's emperor proclaims enthronement in ancient ceremony'], BBC, October 22, 2019.</ref>
== History and Origin ==
The origins of the KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ trace back to the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. Initially formalized by Emperor [[:en:Saga|Saga]] in [[:en:820|820]], the robe's use for specific court rituals and interactions was outlined in the imperial decree recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Kiryaku|Nihon Kiryaku]]. Before this, clothing regulations for emperors were not explicitly stated in earlier codes, such as the [[:en:YЕҚrЕҚ Code|YЕҚrЕҚ Code]].{{Sfn|й»’еқӮ|1939|p=213}}
KЕҚrozen's color was inspired by [[:en:Tang dynasty|Tang Dynasty]] culture but adapted uniquely in Japan. Unlike the {{ill|Zhehuangpao|zh|иөӯй»„иўҚ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhehuangpao|qq=}} worn by Tang emperors, kЕҚrozen is associated with the radiant midday sun.{{sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}} This association is detailed in later writings, such as Takakura NagatsuneвҖҷs ''ZokushinsЕҚ HishЕҚ'' (1288), which states, "The color of the sun is befitting for the Emperor."{{sfn|жӯҰз”°|жҙҘз”°|2016|p=166}}
Since the establishment of the robe's exclusive use for emperors, kЕҚrozen has been regarded as a [[:en:Forbidden colors (Japan)|Forbidden color]]вҖ”strictly prohibited for use by anyone else.{{sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}}
== Design and Construction ==
=== Dyeing Process === According to the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], dyeing the kЕҚrozen fabric required a precise mixture of yamahaze bark and sappanwood. The result was a rich, subdued yellow-brown color, symbolizing the dignity of the Emperor. Due to the complexity of the process, achieving a consistent hue was challenging, even for skilled dyers.{{sfn|еҗүеІЎ|2000}}
=== Patterns and Textures === The robe's patterns have evolved. Initially, designs such as ''Kiritake HЕҚЕҚmon'' (paulownia, bamboo, and phoenix motifs) were used. By the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the design transitioned to rectangular ''kakugata mon'' patterns incorporating mythical creatures like the phoenix and kirin.{{sfn|йҲҙжңЁ|1985}} These patterns remain in use today.
=== Fabric and Lining === Modern KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ robes are made with ''koteaya'' fabric, and their linings vary by tradition.{{sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}}
== Ritual and Usage == The robe is integral to imperial rituals, including the {{ill|Enthronement of the Japanese emperor|ja|еҚідҪҚзӨјжӯЈж®ҝгҒ®е„Җ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enthronement of the Japanese emperor|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, {{ill|Shihohai|lt=Ritual of the Four Directions|ja|еӣӣж–№жӢқ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shihohai|qq=}}, and other key [[:en:Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|Ceremonies of the Japanese Imperial Palace]] ceremonies. Exceptions include the [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]], where the Emperor wears a ceremonial white robe (''gosai fuku'').
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Emperor Showa.jpg|[[:en:1928|1928]] (ShЕҚwa 3), Emperor [[:en:ShЕҚwa|ShЕҚwa]] wearing the KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ during his enthronement ceremony. File:Ceremony of the Enthronement of His Majesty the Emperor at the Seiden7.jpg|2019 (Reiwa 1), Emperor Naruhito during the ''Sokutai Ceremony.'' File:Rittaishi of Prince Naruhito.jpg|Crown Prince Naruhito's investiture ceremony. File:Emperor Takakura.jpg|Portrait of [[:en:Emperor Takakura|Emperor Takakura]], depicted in a KЕҚrozen robe. File:Tang Emperor Taizong.jpg|Tang Emperor Taizong wearing a robe similar in color to KЕҚrozen. </gallery>
<ref></ref> == See Also == * {{ill|Kikujin robe|ja|йәҙеЎөиўҚ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikujin robe|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to йә№еЎөиўҚ}} * {{ill|ЕҢni robe|ja|й»„дё№иўҚ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢni robe|qq=}} * [[:en:Mikado yellow|Mikado yellow]]
== References ==
{{Reflist|2}}
== External Links == * [http://www.iz2.or.jp/fukushoku/f_disp.php?page_no=0000165 "KЕҚrozen Robes"], Costume Museum (replica of modern KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ).
{{translated page|ja|й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ|version=104220377|comment=Imported full ja history}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:KЕҚrozen no gohЕҚ]][[ja:й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ]][[zh:й»„ж Ңжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:KЕҚrozen no gohЕҚ]][[ja:й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ]][[zh:й»„ж Ңжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:KЕҚrozen no gohЕҚ]][[ja:й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ]][[zh:й»„ж Ңжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11677782]] Wikidata name: KЕҚrozen no gohЕҚ [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Emperor Naruhito (cropped).jpg|thumb]]'''KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ''' (й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ) is a [[:en:Sokutai|sokutai]] ceremonial robe worn exclusively by the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]] during enthronement ceremonies and important rituals. It is a highly symbolic robe, dyed in the unique color known as {{ill|KЕҚrozen|ja|й»„ж«Ёжҹ“|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KЕҚrozen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ}}, reserved solely for the Emperor.
The color kЕҚrozen is derived from natural dyes, specifically the bark of the {{ill|Yamahaze|ja|еұұж«Ё|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamahaze|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} tree and the heartwood of {{ill|Sappanwood|ja|иҳҮиҠі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sappanwood|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮ№гӮӘгӮҰ|1=Biancaea sappan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56088062}}. It has been described as a "reddish-yellow"{{Sfn|ж—Ҙжң¬еӣҪиӘһеӨ§иҫһе…ё|2000}} or "yellowish-brown."{{Sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}} Variations in tone are said to reflect differences in era and the wearer's age.{{Sfn|йҲҙжңЁ|1985}} In 2019, during the [[:en:2019_Japanese_imperial_transition#Enthronement_Ceremony|Sokutai Ceremony]], foreign media described the color as "brown-gold." <ref>[https://www.bbc.com/news/world-asia-50120563 'Naruhito: Japan's emperor proclaims enthronement in ancient ceremony'], BBC, October 22, 2019.</ref>
== History and Origin ==
The origins of the KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ trace back to the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. Initially formalized by Emperor [[:en:Saga|Saga]] in [[:en:820|820]], the robe's use for specific court rituals and interactions was outlined in the imperial decree recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Kiryaku|Nihon Kiryaku]]. Before this, clothing regulations for emperors were not explicitly stated in earlier codes, such as the [[:en:YЕҚrЕҚ Code|YЕҚrЕҚ Code]].{{Sfn|й»’еқӮ|1939|p=213}}
KЕҚrozen's color was inspired by [[:en:Tang dynasty|Tang Dynasty]] culture but adapted uniquely in Japan. Unlike the {{ill|Zhehuangpao|zh|иөӯй»„иўҚ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhehuangpao|qq=}} worn by Tang emperors, kЕҚrozen is associated with the radiant midday sun.{{sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}} This association is detailed in later writings, such as Takakura NagatsuneвҖҷs ''ZokushinsЕҚ HishЕҚ'' (1288), which states, "The color of the sun is befitting for the Emperor."{{sfn|жӯҰз”°|жҙҘз”°|2016|p=166}}
Since the establishment of the robe's exclusive use for emperors, kЕҚrozen has been regarded as a [[:en:Forbidden colors (Japan)|Forbidden color]]вҖ”strictly prohibited for use by anyone else.{{sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}}
== Design and Construction ==
=== Dyeing Process === According to the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], dyeing the kЕҚrozen fabric required a precise mixture of yamahaze bark and sappanwood. The result was a rich, subdued yellow-brown color, symbolizing the dignity of the Emperor. Due to the complexity of the process, achieving a consistent hue was challenging, even for skilled dyers.{{sfn|еҗүеІЎ|2000}}
=== Patterns and Textures === The robe's patterns have evolved. Initially, designs such as ''Kiritake HЕҚЕҚmon'' (paulownia, bamboo, and phoenix motifs) were used. By the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the design transitioned to rectangular ''kakugata mon'' patterns incorporating mythical creatures like the phoenix and kirin.{{sfn|йҲҙжңЁ|1985}} These patterns remain in use today.
=== Fabric and Lining === Modern KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ robes are made with ''koteaya'' fabric, and their linings vary by tradition.{{sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}}
== Ritual and Usage == The robe is integral to imperial rituals, including the {{ill|Enthronement of the Japanese emperor|ja|еҚідҪҚзӨјжӯЈж®ҝгҒ®е„Җ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enthronement of the Japanese emperor|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, {{ill|Shihohai|lt=Ritual of the Four Directions|ja|еӣӣж–№жӢқ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shihohai|qq=}}, and other key [[:en:Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|Ceremonies of the Japanese Imperial Palace]] ceremonies. Exceptions include the [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]], where the Emperor wears a ceremonial white robe (''gosai fuku'').
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Emperor Showa.jpg|[[:en:1928|1928]] (ShЕҚwa 3), Emperor [[:en:ShЕҚwa|ShЕҚwa]] wearing the KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ during his enthronement ceremony. File:Ceremony of the Enthronement of His Majesty the Emperor at the Seiden7.jpg|2019 (Reiwa 1), Emperor Naruhito during the ''Sokutai Ceremony.'' File:Rittaishi of Prince Naruhito.jpg|Crown Prince Naruhito's investiture ceremony. File:Emperor Takakura.jpg|Portrait of [[:en:Emperor Takakura|Emperor Takakura]], depicted in a KЕҚrozen robe. File:Tang Emperor Taizong.jpg|Tang Emperor Taizong wearing a robe similar in color to KЕҚrozen. </gallery>
<ref></ref> == See Also == * {{ill|Kikujin robe|ja|йәҙеЎөиўҚ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikujin robe|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to йә№еЎөиўҚ}} * {{ill|ЕҢni robe|ja|й»„дё№иўҚ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢni robe|qq=}} * [[:en:Mikado yellow|Mikado yellow]]

<!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|KЕҚrozen no gohЕҚ}} [[zh:й»„ж Ңжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ]] {{wikidata link|Q11677782}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Traditional clothing of the Heian period]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:KЕҚrozen no gohЕҚ]][[ja:й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ]][[zh:й»„ж Ңжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:KЕҚrozen no gohЕҚ]][[ja:й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ]][[zh:й»„ж Ңжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:KЕҚrozen no gohЕҚ]][[ja:й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ]][[zh:й»„ж Ңжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ]]
[[File:Emperor Naruhito (cropped).jpg|thumb]]'''KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ''' (й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ) is a {{ill|Sokutai|en|Sokutai|WD=Q1156828|lt=sokutai}} ceremonial robe worn exclusively by the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=Emperor of Japan}} during enthronement ceremonies and important rituals. It is a highly symbolic robe, dyed in the unique color known as {{ill|KЕҚrozen|ja|й»„ж«Ёжҹ“|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KЕҚrozen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ}}, reserved solely for the Emperor.
The color kЕҚrozen is derived from natural dyes, specifically the bark of the {{ill|Yamahaze|ja|еұұж«Ё|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamahaze|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} tree and the heartwood of {{ill|Sappanwood|ja|иҳҮиҠі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sappanwood|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮ№гӮӘгӮҰ|1=Biancaea sappan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56088062}}. It has been described as a "reddish-yellow"{{Sfn|ж—Ҙжң¬еӣҪиӘһеӨ§иҫһе…ё|2000}} or "yellowish-brown."{{Sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}} Variations in tone are said to reflect differences in era and the wearer's age.{{Sfn|йҲҙжңЁ|1985}} In 2019, during the {{ill|2019_Japanese_imperial_transition#Enthronement_Ceremony|en|2019_Japanese_imperial_transition#Enthronement_Ceremony|WD=Q44023338|lt=Sokutai Ceremony}}, foreign media described the color as "brown-gold." <ref>[https://www.bbc.com/news/world-asia-50120563 'Naruhito: Japan's emperor proclaims enthronement in ancient ceremony'], BBC, October 22, 2019.</ref>
== History and Origin ==
The origins of the KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ trace back to the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}. Initially formalized by Emperor {{ill|Saga|en|Saga|WD=Q180494|lt=Saga}} in {{ill|820|en|820|WD=Q24872|lt=820}}, the robe's use for specific court rituals and interactions was outlined in the imperial decree recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|en|Nihon Kiryaku|WD=Q1053542|lt=Nihon Kiryaku}}. Before this, clothing regulations for emperors were not explicitly stated in earlier codes, such as the {{ill|YЕҚrЕҚ Code|en|YЕҚrЕҚ Code|WD=Q2575743|lt=YЕҚrЕҚ Code}}.{{Sfn|й»’еқӮ|1939|p=213}}
KЕҚrozen's color was inspired by {{ill|Tang dynasty|en|Tang dynasty|WD=Q9683|lt=Tang Dynasty}} culture but adapted uniquely in Japan. Unlike the {{ill|Zhehuangpao|zh|иөӯй»„иўҚ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhehuangpao|qq=}} worn by Tang emperors, kЕҚrozen is associated with the radiant midday sun.{{sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}} This association is detailed in later writings, such as Takakura NagatsuneвҖҷs ''ZokushinsЕҚ HishЕҚ'' (1288), which states, "The color of the sun is befitting for the Emperor."{{sfn|жӯҰз”°|жҙҘз”°|2016|p=166}}
Since the establishment of the robe's exclusive use for emperors, kЕҚrozen has been regarded as a {{ill|Forbidden colors (Japan)|en|Forbidden colors (Japan)|WD=Q11591157|lt=Forbidden color}}вҖ”strictly prohibited for use by anyone else.{{sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}}
== Design and Construction ==
=== Dyeing Process === According to the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}}, dyeing the kЕҚrozen fabric required a precise mixture of yamahaze bark and sappanwood. The result was a rich, subdued yellow-brown color, symbolizing the dignity of the Emperor. Due to the complexity of the process, achieving a consistent hue was challenging, even for skilled dyers.{{sfn|еҗүеІЎ|2000}}
=== Patterns and Textures === The robe's patterns have evolved. Initially, designs such as ''Kiritake HЕҚЕҚmon'' (paulownia, bamboo, and phoenix motifs) were used. By the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}}, the design transitioned to rectangular ''kakugata mon'' patterns incorporating mythical creatures like the phoenix and kirin.{{sfn|йҲҙжңЁ|1985}} These patterns remain in use today.
=== Fabric and Lining === Modern KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ robes are made with ''koteaya'' fabric, and their linings vary by tradition.{{sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}}
== Ritual and Usage == The robe is integral to imperial rituals, including the {{ill|Enthronement of the Japanese emperor|ja|еҚідҪҚзӨјжӯЈж®ҝгҒ®е„Җ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enthronement of the Japanese emperor|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, {{ill|Shihohai|lt=Ritual of the Four Directions|ja|еӣӣж–№жӢқ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shihohai|qq=}}, and other key {{ill|Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|en|Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|WD=Q11453226|lt=Ceremonies of the Japanese Imperial Palace}} ceremonies. Exceptions include the {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niinamesai}}, where the Emperor wears a ceremonial white robe (''gosai fuku'').
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Emperor Showa.jpg|{{ill|1928|en|1928|WD=Q19715|lt=1928}} (ShЕҚwa 3), Emperor {{ill|ShЕҚwa|en|ShЕҚwa|WD=Q398996|lt=ShЕҚwa}} wearing the KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ during his enthronement ceremony. File:Ceremony of the Enthronement of His Majesty the Emperor at the Seiden7.jpg|2019 (Reiwa 1), Emperor Naruhito during the ''Sokutai Ceremony.'' File:Rittaishi of Prince Naruhito.jpg|Crown Prince Naruhito's investiture ceremony. File:Emperor Takakura.jpg|Portrait of {{ill|Emperor Takakura|en|Emperor Takakura|WD=Q357261|lt=Emperor Takakura}}, depicted in a KЕҚrozen robe. File:Tang Emperor Taizong.jpg|Tang Emperor Taizong wearing a robe similar in color to KЕҚrozen. </gallery>
<ref></ref> == See Also == * {{ill|Kikujin robe|ja|йәҙеЎөиўҚ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikujin robe|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to йә№еЎөиўҚ}} * {{ill|ЕҢni robe|ja|й»„дё№иўҚ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢni robe|qq=}} * {{ill|Mikado yellow|en|Mikado yellow|WD=Q6845574|lt=Mikado yellow}}

File:Tang Emperor Taizong.jpg|Tang Emperor Taizong wearing a robe similar in color to KЕҚrozen. </gallery>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:KЕҚrozen no gohЕҚ]][[ja:й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ]][[zh:й»„ж Ңжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:KЕҚrozen no gohЕҚ]][[ja:й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ]][[zh:й»„ж Ңжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:KЕҚrozen no gohЕҚ]][[ja:й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ]][[zh:й»„ж Ңжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ]]
[[File:Emperor Naruhito (cropped).jpg|thumb]]'''KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ''' (й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ) is a {{ill|Sokutai|en|Sokutai|WD=Q1156828|lt=sokutai}} ceremonial robe worn exclusively by the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=Emperor of Japan}} during enthronement ceremonies and important rituals. It is a highly symbolic robe, dyed in the unique color known as {{ill|KЕҚrozen|ja|й»„ж«Ёжҹ“|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KЕҚrozen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ}}, reserved solely for the Emperor.
The color kЕҚrozen is derived from natural dyes, specifically the bark of the {{ill|Yamahaze|ja|еұұж«Ё|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamahaze|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} tree and the heartwood of {{ill|Sappanwood|ja|иҳҮиҠі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sappanwood|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮ№гӮӘгӮҰ|1=Biancaea sappan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56088062}}. It has been described as a "reddish-yellow"{{Sfn|ж—Ҙжң¬еӣҪиӘһеӨ§иҫһе…ё|2000}} or "yellowish-brown."{{Sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}} Variations in tone are said to reflect differences in era and the wearer's age.{{Sfn|йҲҙжңЁ|1985}} In 2019, during the {{ill|2019_Japanese_imperial_transition#Enthronement_Ceremony|en|2019_Japanese_imperial_transition#Enthronement_Ceremony|WD=Q44023338|lt=Sokutai Ceremony}}, foreign media described the color as "brown-gold." <ref>[https://www.bbc.com/news/world-asia-50120563 'Naruhito: Japan's emperor proclaims enthronement in ancient ceremony'], BBC, October 22, 2019.</ref>
== History and Origin ==
The origins of the KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ trace back to the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}. Initially formalized by Emperor {{ill|Saga|en|Saga|WD=Q180494|lt=Saga}} in {{ill|820|en|820|WD=Q24872|lt=820}}, the robe's use for specific court rituals and interactions was outlined in the imperial decree recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|en|Nihon Kiryaku|WD=Q1053542|lt=Nihon Kiryaku}}. Before this, clothing regulations for emperors were not explicitly stated in earlier codes, such as the {{ill|YЕҚrЕҚ Code|en|YЕҚrЕҚ Code|WD=Q2575743|lt=YЕҚrЕҚ Code}}.{{Sfn|й»’еқӮ|1939|p=213}}
KЕҚrozen's color was inspired by {{ill|Tang dynasty|en|Tang dynasty|WD=Q9683|lt=Tang Dynasty}} culture but adapted uniquely in Japan. Unlike the {{ill|Zhehuangpao|zh|иөӯй»„иўҚ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhehuangpao|qq=}} worn by Tang emperors, kЕҚrozen is associated with the radiant midday sun.{{sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}} This association is detailed in later writings, such as Takakura NagatsuneвҖҷs ''ZokushinsЕҚ HishЕҚ'' (1288), which states, "The color of the sun is befitting for the Emperor."{{sfn|жӯҰз”°|жҙҘз”°|2016|p=166}}
Since the establishment of the robe's exclusive use for emperors, kЕҚrozen has been regarded as a {{ill|Forbidden colors (Japan)|en|Forbidden colors (Japan)|WD=Q11591157|lt=Forbidden color}}вҖ”strictly prohibited for use by anyone else.{{sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}}
== Design and Construction ==
=== Dyeing Process === According to the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}}, dyeing the kЕҚrozen fabric required a precise mixture of yamahaze bark and sappanwood. The result was a rich, subdued yellow-brown color, symbolizing the dignity of the Emperor. Due to the complexity of the process, achieving a consistent hue was challenging, even for skilled dyers.{{sfn|еҗүеІЎ|2000}}
=== Patterns and Textures === The robe's patterns have evolved. Initially, designs such as ''Kiritake HЕҚЕҚmon'' (paulownia, bamboo, and phoenix motifs) were used. By the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}}, the design transitioned to rectangular ''kakugata mon'' patterns incorporating mythical creatures like the phoenix and kirin.{{sfn|йҲҙжңЁ|1985}} These patterns remain in use today.
=== Fabric and Lining === Modern KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ robes are made with ''koteaya'' fabric, and their linings vary by tradition.{{sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}}
== Ritual and Usage == The robe is integral to imperial rituals, including the {{ill|Enthronement of the Japanese emperor|ja|еҚідҪҚзӨјжӯЈж®ҝгҒ®е„Җ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enthronement of the Japanese emperor|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, {{ill|Shihohai|lt=Ritual of the Four Directions|ja|еӣӣж–№жӢқ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shihohai|qq=}}, and other key {{ill|Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|en|Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|WD=Q11453226|lt=Ceremonies of the Japanese Imperial Palace}} ceremonies. Exceptions include the {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niinamesai}}, where the Emperor wears a ceremonial white robe (''gosai fuku'').
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Emperor Showa.jpg|{{ill|1928|en|1928|WD=Q19715|lt=1928}} (ShЕҚwa 3), Emperor {{ill|ShЕҚwa|en|ShЕҚwa|WD=Q398996|lt=ShЕҚwa}} wearing the KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ during his enthronement ceremony. File:Ceremony of the Enthronement of His Majesty the Emperor at the Seiden7.jpg|2019 (Reiwa 1), Emperor Naruhito during the ''Sokutai Ceremony.'' File:Rittaishi of Prince Naruhito.jpg|Crown Prince Naruhito's investiture ceremony. File:Emperor Takakura.jpg|Portrait of {{ill|Emperor Takakura|en|Emperor Takakura|WD=Q357261|lt=Emperor Takakura}}, depicted in a KЕҚrozen robe. File:Tang Emperor Taizong.jpg|Tang Emperor Taizong wearing a robe similar in color to KЕҚrozen. </gallery>
<ref></ref>
== See Also == * {{ill|Kikujin robe|ja|йәҙеЎөиўҚ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikujin robe|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to йә№еЎөиўҚ}} * {{ill|ЕҢni robe|ja|й»„дё№иўҚ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢni robe|qq=}} * {{ill|Mikado yellow|en|Mikado yellow|WD=Q6845574|lt=Mikado yellow}}

By the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}, the design transitioned to rectangular ''kakugata mon'' patterns incorporating mythical creatures like the phoenix and kirin.{{sfn|йҲҙжңЁ|1985}} These patterns remain in use today.
=== Fabric and Lining === Modern KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ robes are made with ''koteaya'' fabric, and their linings vary by tradition.{{sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}}
== Ritual and Usage == The robe is integral to imperial rituals, including the {{ill|Enthronement of the Japanese emperor|ja|еҚідҪҚзӨјжӯЈж®ҝгҒ®е„Җ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enthronement of the Japanese emperor|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, {{ill|Shihohai|lt=Ritual of the Four Directions|ja|еӣӣж–№жӢқ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shihohai|qq=}}, and other key {{ill|Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|en|Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|WD=Q11453226|lt=Ceremonies of the Japanese Imperial Palace|1=Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace}} ceremonies. Exceptions include the {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niinamesai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}, where the Emperor wears a ceremonial white robe (''gosai fuku'').
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Emperor Showa.jpg|{{ill|1928|en|1928|WD=Q19715|lt=1928|1=1928}} (ShЕҚwa 3), Emperor {{ill|ShЕҚwa|en|ShЕҚwa|WD=Q398996|lt=ShЕҚwa|1=ShЕҚwa}} wearing the KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ during his enthronement ceremony. File:Ceremony of the Enthronement of His Majesty the Emperor at the Seiden7.jpg|2019 (Reiwa 1), Emperor Naruhito during the ''Sokutai Ceremony.'' File:Rittaishi of Prince Naruhito.jpg|Crown Prince Naruhito's investiture ceremony. File:Emperor Takakura.jpg|Portrait of {{ill|Emperor Takakura|en|Emperor Takakura|WD=Q357261|lt=Emperor Takakura|1=Emperor Takakura}}, depicted in a KЕҚrozen robe. File:Tang Emperor Taizong.jpg|Tang Emperor Taizong wearing a robe similar in color to KЕҚrozen. </gallery>
<ref></ref>

<!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|KЕҚrozen no gohЕҚ}} [[zh:й»„ж Ңжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ]] {{wikidata link|Q11677782}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Traditional clothing of the Heian period]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:KЕҚrozen no gohЕҚ]][[ja:й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ]][[zh:й»„ж Ңжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:KЕҚrozen no gohЕҚ]][[ja:й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ]][[zh:й»„ж Ңжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|commons:Category:KЕҚrozen no gohЕҚ]][[ja:й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ]][[zh:й»„ж Ңжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ]]
[[File:Emperor Naruhito (cropped).jpg|thumb]]'''KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ''' (й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ) is a {{ill|Sokutai|en|Sokutai|WD=Q1156828|lt=sokutai|1=Sokutai}} ceremonial robe worn exclusively by the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=Emperor of Japan|1=Emperor of Japan}} during enthronement ceremonies and important rituals. It is a highly symbolic robe, dyed in the unique color known as {{ill|KЕҚrozen|ja|й»„ж«Ёжҹ“|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KЕҚrozen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ}}, reserved solely for the Emperor.
The color kЕҚrozen is derived from natural dyes, specifically the bark of the {{ill|Yamahaze|ja|еұұж«Ё|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamahaze|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} tree and the heartwood of {{ill|Sappanwood|ja|иҳҮиҠі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sappanwood|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮ№гӮӘгӮҰ|1=Biancaea sappan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56088062}}. It has been described as a "reddish-yellow"{{Sfn|ж—Ҙжң¬еӣҪиӘһеӨ§иҫһе…ё|2000}} or "yellowish-brown."{{Sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}} Variations in tone are said to reflect differences in era and the wearer's age.{{Sfn|йҲҙжңЁ|1985}} In 2019, during the {{ill|2019_Japanese_imperial_transition#Enthronement_Ceremony|en|2019_Japanese_imperial_transition#Enthronement_Ceremony|WD=Q44023338|lt=Sokutai Ceremony|1=2019 Japanese imperial transition}}, foreign media described the color as "brown-gold." <ref>[https://www.bbc.com/news/world-asia-50120563 'Naruhito: Japan's emperor proclaims enthronement in ancient ceremony'], BBC, October 22, 2019.</ref>
== History and Origin ==
The origins of the KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ trace back to the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. Initially formalized by Emperor {{ill|Saga|en|Saga|WD=Q180494|lt=Saga|1=Saga}} in {{ill|820|en|820|WD=Q24872|lt=820|1=820}}, the robe's use for specific court rituals and interactions was outlined in the imperial decree recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|en|Nihon Kiryaku|WD=Q1053542|lt=Nihon Kiryaku|1=Nihon Kiryaku}}. Before this, clothing regulations for emperors were not explicitly stated in earlier codes, such as the {{ill|YЕҚrЕҚ Code|en|YЕҚrЕҚ Code|WD=Q2575743|lt=YЕҚrЕҚ Code|1=YЕҚrЕҚ Code}}.{{Sfn|й»’еқӮ|1939|p=213}}
KЕҚrozen's color was inspired by {{ill|Tang dynasty|en|Tang dynasty|WD=Q9683|lt=Tang Dynasty|1=Tang dynasty}} culture but adapted uniquely in Japan. Unlike the {{ill|Zhehuangpao|zh|иөӯй»„иўҚ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhehuangpao|qq=}} worn by Tang emperors, kЕҚrozen is associated with the radiant midday sun.{{sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}} This association is detailed in later writings, such as Takakura NagatsuneвҖҷs ''ZokushinsЕҚ HishЕҚ'' (1288), which states, "The color of the sun is befitting for the Emperor."{{sfn|жӯҰз”°|жҙҘз”°|2016|p=166}}
Since the establishment of the robe's exclusive use for emperors, kЕҚrozen has been regarded as a {{ill|Forbidden colors (Japan)|en|Forbidden colors (Japan)|WD=Q11591157|lt=Forbidden color|1=Forbidden colors (Japan)}}вҖ”strictly prohibited for use by anyone else.{{sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}}
== Design and Construction ==
=== Dyeing Process === According to the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}, dyeing the kЕҚrozen fabric required a precise mixture of yamahaze bark and sappanwood. The result was a rich, subdued yellow-brown color, symbolizing the dignity of the Emperor. Due to the complexity of the process, achieving a consistent hue was challenging, even for skilled dyers.{{sfn|еҗүеІЎ|2000}}
=== Patterns and Textures === The robe's patterns have evolved. Initially, designs such as ''Kiritake HЕҚЕҚmon'' (paulownia, bamboo, and phoenix motifs) were used. By the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}, the design transitioned to rectangular ''kakugata mon'' patterns incorporating mythical creatures like the phoenix and kirin.{{sfn|йҲҙжңЁ|1985}} These patterns remain in use today.
=== Fabric and Lining === Modern KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ robes are made with ''koteaya'' fabric, and their linings vary by tradition.{{sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}}
== Ritual and Usage == The robe is integral to imperial rituals, including the {{ill|Enthronement of the Japanese emperor|ja|еҚідҪҚзӨјжӯЈж®ҝгҒ®е„Җ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enthronement of the Japanese emperor|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, {{ill|Shihohai|lt=Ritual of the Four Directions|ja|еӣӣж–№жӢқ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shihohai|qq=}}, and other key {{ill|Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|en|Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|WD=Q11453226|lt=Ceremonies of the Japanese Imperial Palace|1=Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace}} ceremonies. Exceptions include the {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niinamesai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}, where the Emperor wears a ceremonial white robe (''gosai fuku'').
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Emperor Showa.jpg|{{ill|1928|en|1928|WD=Q19715|lt=1928|1=1928}} (ShЕҚwa 3), Emperor {{ill|ShЕҚwa|en|ShЕҚwa|WD=Q398996|lt=ShЕҚwa|1=ShЕҚwa}} wearing the KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ during his enthronement ceremony. File:Ceremony of the Enthronement of His Majesty the Emperor at the Seiden7.jpg|2019 (Reiwa 1), Emperor Naruhito during the ''Sokutai Ceremony.'' File:Rittaishi of Prince Naruhito.jpg|Crown Prince Naruhito's investiture ceremony. File:Emperor Takakura.jpg|Portrait of {{ill|Emperor Takakura|en|Emperor Takakura|WD=Q357261|lt=Emperor Takakura|1=Emperor Takakura}}, depicted in a KЕҚrozen robe. File:Tang Emperor Taizong.jpg|Tang Emperor Taizong wearing a robe similar in color to KЕҚrozen. </gallery>
<ref></ref>
== See Also == * {{ill|Kikujin robe|ja|йәҙеЎөиўҚ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikujin robe|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to йә№еЎөиўҚ}} * {{ill|ЕҢni robe|ja|й»„дё№иўҚ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢni robe|qq=}} * {{ill|Mikado yellow|en|Mikado yellow|WD=Q6845574|lt=Mikado yellow|1=Mikado yellow}}

== History and Origin ==
The origins of the KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ trace back to the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. Initially formalized by Emperor {{ill|Saga|en|Saga|WD=Q180494|lt=Saga|1=Saga}} in {{ill|820|en|820|WD=Q24872|lt=820|1=820}}, the robe's use for specific court rituals and interactions was outlined in the imperial decree recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|en|Nihon Kiryaku|WD=Q1053542|lt=Nihon Kiryaku|1=Nihon Kiryaku}}. Before this, clothing regulations for emperors were not explicitly stated in earlier codes, such as the {{ill|YЕҚrЕҚ Code|en|YЕҚrЕҚ Code|WD=Q2575743|lt=YЕҚrЕҚ Code|1=YЕҚrЕҚ Code}}.{{Sfn|й»’еқӮ|1939|p=213}}
KЕҚrozen's color was inspired by {{ill|Tang dynasty|en|Tang dynasty|WD=Q9683|lt=Tang Dynasty|1=Tang dynasty}} culture but adapted uniquely in Japan. Unlike the {{ill|Zhehuangpao|zh|иөӯй»„иўҚ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhehuangpao|qq=}} worn by Tang emperors, kЕҚrozen is associated with the radiant midday sun.{{sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}} This association is detailed in later writings, such as Takakura NagatsuneвҖҷs ''ZokushinsЕҚ HishЕҚ'' (1288), which states, "The color of the sun is befitting for the Emperor."{{sfn|жӯҰз”°|жҙҘз”°|2016|p=166}}
Since the establishment of the robe's exclusive use for emperors, kЕҚrozen has been regarded as a {{ill|Forbidden colors (Japan)|en|Forbidden colors (Japan)|WD=Q11591157|lt=Forbidden color|1=Forbidden colors (Japan)}}вҖ”strictly prohibited for use by anyone else.{{sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}}
== Design and Construction ==
=== Dyeing Process === According to the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}, dyeing the kЕҚrozen fabric required a precise mixture of yamahaze bark and sappanwood. The result was a rich, subdued yellow-brown color, symbolizing the dignity of the Emperor. Due to the complexity of the process, achieving a consistent hue was challenging, even for skilled dyers.{{sfn|еҗүеІЎ|2000}}
=== Patterns and Textures === The robe's patterns have evolved. Initially, designs such as ''Kiritake HЕҚЕҚmon'' (paulownia, bamboo, and phoenix motifs) were used. By the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}, the design transitioned to rectangular ''kakugata mon'' patterns incorporating mythical creatures like the phoenix and kirin.{{sfn|йҲҙжңЁ|1985}} These patterns remain in use today.
=== Fabric and Lining === Modern KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ robes are made with ''koteaya'' fabric, and their linings vary by tradition.{{sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}}
== Ritual and Usage == The robe is integral to imperial rituals, including the {{ill|Enthronement of the Japanese emperor|ja|еҚідҪҚзӨјжӯЈж®ҝгҒ®е„Җ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enthronement of the Japanese emperor|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, {{ill|Shihohai|lt=Ritual of the Four Directions|ja|еӣӣж–№жӢқ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shihohai|qq=}}, and other key {{ill|Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|en|Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|WD=Q11453226|lt=Ceremonies of the Japanese Imperial Palace|1=Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace}} ceremonies. Exceptions include the {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niinamesai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}, where the Emperor wears a ceremonial white robe (''gosai fuku'').

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:KЕҚrozen no gohЕҚ]][[ja:й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ]][[zh:й»„ж Ңжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:KЕҚrozen no gohЕҚ]][[ja:й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ]][[zh:й»„ж Ңжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ]][[zh:й»„ж Ңжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ]]
[[File:Emperor Naruhito (cropped).jpg|thumb]]'''KЕҚrozen no GohЕҚ''' (й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ) is a {{ill|Sokutai|en|Sokutai|qid=Q1156828|lt=sokutai|1=Sokutai}} ceremonial robe worn exclusively by the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|qid=Q208233|lt=Emperor of Japan|1=Emperor of Japan}} during enthronement ceremonies and important rituals. It is a highly symbolic robe, dyed in the unique color known as {{ill|KЕҚrozen|ja|й»„ж«Ёжҹ“|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KЕҚrozen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to й»„ж«Ёжҹ“еҫЎиўҚ}}, reserved solely for the Emperor.
The color kЕҚrozen is derived from natural dyes, specifically the bark of the {{ill|Yamahaze|ja|еұұж«Ё|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamahaze|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} tree and the heartwood of {{ill|Sappanwood|ja|иҳҮиҠі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sappanwood|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮ№гӮӘгӮҰ|1=Biancaea sappan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q56088062}}. It has been described as a "reddish-yellow"{{Sfn|ж—Ҙжң¬еӣҪиӘһеӨ§иҫһе…ё|2000}} or "yellowish-brown."{{Sfn|й«ҳз”°|1986}} Variations in tone are said to reflect differences in era and the wearer's age.{{Sfn|йҲҙжңЁ|1985}} In 2019, during the {{ill|2019_Japanese_imperial_transition#Enthronement_Ceremony|en|2019_Japanese_imperial_transition#Enthronement_Ceremony|qid=Q44023338|lt=Sokutai Ceremony|1=2019 Japanese imperial transition}}, foreign media described the color as "brown-gold." <ref>[https://www.bbc.com/news/world-asia-50120563 'Naruhito: Japan's emperor proclaims enthronement in ancient ceremony'], BBC, October 22, 2019.</ref>
